programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of a marine is found strung up at a construction site, the agents travel to LA to work with another NCIS team on an investigation into an international arms deal that escalates into a high-priority case, and Tony begins to pry into Ziva's life. Guest starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J (NCIS: Los Angeles)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble becomes involved in a personal problem concerning his boss and boss's wife while working on a construction project

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman is accused of poisoning some men after an ex-con and his pals try to swindle their way into a lucrative exotic orchid business via a poker game

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a Navy captain is killed in a robbery that went badly wrong, Gibbs' former mother-in-law surfaces as a witness in the investigation, and it transpires the victim had ties to a drug cartel that murdered the agent's first wife Shannon and daughter Kelly. Guest starring Gena Rowlands

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker implements a gruelling six-week boot camp programme in an attempt to rehabilitate a group of hardened criminals. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. Forensic scientist Abby Sciuto travels from the NCIS office in Washington, DC to help out as the case has similarities to a serial killer she has been tracking. Guest starring Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine is found with a gunshot wound six years after disappearing in Afghanistan, and the team discovers he vanished while carrying out an undercover operation. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The baby's body is found and evidence stacks up against the nanny's boyfriend, but Sam's gentle interrogation of the parents produces another startling suspect. Eventually, toxicology reports on the victim present a stark truth demanding all the DI's sensitivity and tact as she helps the grieving mother face up to what happened

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Hanssen's plan to secure Holby's future as a teaching hospital runs into trouble when he clashes with the university's vice-chancellor, Philippa Pawlowski, about who should be appointed clinical skills teacher. Elliot faces a dilemma after being told he cannot treat a critically ill Ukrainian patient, and Sacha is forced to reveal his secret to Chrissie

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tess and Nadia reluctantly make their way to the party together, only to be confronted by the sight of a burning man in the road. Old demons resurface when Nadia tries to comfort the victim's traumatised son, while a prank on Nathan takes a dramatic turn as the celebration gets under way. Geoffrey Hughes guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a talented young athlete is found battered to death in a park, officers interrogate fellow guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening. His schoolmates suggest gatecrashers bringing drugs into the house may have been responsible, but their versions of events reveal inconsistencies and suggest a completely different explanation

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol and Alan get a visit from the police who say they know the gang who threatened Carol. They want Carol to identify the gang at the police station

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Huw and Robbie are smitten with Mary. Irene and Terry flirt. Carol and Alan are introduced to their Victim Liaison Officer. Diane gets a supermarket job

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth decides to organise a formal party to demonstrate how out of her depth Sarah is. Meanwhile, Carolyn is shocked by Jack's revelation when she challenges him to confront his intimacy issues, and Regina returns home from Europe with news about Sarah. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The Jersey detective is shocked when he returns from sick leave to find his job at stake, and a colleague is murdered when investigating international gun sales. Crime drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: The doctors discover a bogus GP has been making house calls to Paul's patients under his name and call in police to investigate. The medic worries his reputation could be tarnished permanently - especially as the imposter seems to know his every move. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey invites an old school friend to stay for a few days, leaving Sharon out in the cold. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Quite a few feathers are ruffled as Joe Maplin pays a secret visit to the camp and decides Gladys should be promoted to head Yellowcoat - prompting her to sew on some stripes to display her superior rank. With Ruth Madoc, Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Although Emmet's musical is already fully cast, Hyacinth remains convinced that his shyness is the only reason she has not been given a starring role. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, David Griffin and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley demonstrates his new hand-crafted trolley, complete with deluxe car seat - guaranteed to bring back childhood memories to the dispirited Smiler. Meanwhile, Barry gets into a tangle when he takes up yoga. Gordon Wharmby, Stephen Lewis and Mike Grady star

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Celebrity portrait painter Agatha Troy asks Alleyn to investigate the death of a venerable Shakespearean actor whose portrait she had been commissioned to paint. Drama, starring Patrick Malahide, with Belinda Lang

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A recent rape is linked to two similar attacks at a chocolate factory 10 years previously, leaving the team with no choice but to reopen all three cases. The detectives uncover news not only of an unhappy workforce, but also of an alleged suicide, and their investigation becomes even more complicated when they discover a severed finger was found in one of the company's snacks. After a visit to the doctor, Gerry decides to quit smoking, and instead of resorting to nicotine patches or gum, he enlists Sandra to help him quit

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker is assigned to investigate the discovery of a young woman's skeleton, found underneath the patio of what used to be a boarding house, but is paired with Pat's ex-boyfriend Jeff. Drama from Widows creator Lynda LaPlante, starring David Hayman and Paul Kynman

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Tensions rise in Pagford as the parish council election approaches and each side steps up its campaign, with the fate of Sweetlove House hanging in the balance. Meanwhile, Miles pines for a quiet life and promises Samantha that once the vote is over everything will calm down, but she doesn't believe him, and Howard's dark secret is revealed in the Ghost's final post. Krystal returns home to find Terri back to her old ways, Hope seems more fragile than ever and news of a tragedy leaves the whole community reeling

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is shocked when his mentor from 2006 arrives at the station, and offers his advice when the new recruit becomes the butt of Ray's racist jokes. Meanwhile, Gene aims to impress Supt Woolf by finding the person responsible for a spate of armed robberies, but the investigation gets off to a shaky start. Ray Emmet Brown guest stars

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Peggy has to watch her step caring for a woman whose family don't want her to know she is dying. Meanwhile, a Danish delegation arrives at the factory in anticipation of signing a contract with Simon, but they're unimpressed with the set-up _ until a spot of good old Yorkshire hospitality saves the day. Sentimental drama, with Pam Ferris and Sarah Lancashire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a talented young athlete is found battered to death in a park, officers interrogate fellow guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Enthusiastic new student doctor Tara is keen to get involved when she starts work on the ward - but ends up putting a patient's life in danger. Eddi discovers a different side to Luc's personality when a friend of his arrives seeking treatment, while Chantelle breaks a promise to Lleucu, leaving her colleague in serious financial trouble

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A binman is crushed by his lorry after rescuing a baby discarded with the rubbish, and a teenager is admitted with a post-partum haemorrhage - much to her mother's disbelief. Selena's past catches up with her and begins to affect her work as she and Nathan find themselves the subject of gossip, while Stitch plays Cupid for Guppy. Former Emmerdale star Emma Atkins guests

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton and Meadows play a psychological game, putting pressure on the teenage partygoers by staging a reconstruction and holding a press conference to rattle the composure of the suspects - aiming to uncover the truth about the death of the young athlete. Eddie finds a piece of wood which he believes is the murder weapon, but the nail that caused the puncture wound in the victim's spine is missing

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie wins a boxing match. Joe wins a pool match. The Jackson's are being intimidated

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Jessie comes home but is unresponsive. Joe proposes marriage to Sarah. Carol's fears about protecting her family come true when Billy is bundled off into a van

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah is closer to George than ever after her triumph at the Swansons' party. However, their happiness is short-lived when they are faced with Regina's bombshell, which sends shockwaves through Inverness. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: A charity boss with a taste for the high life is found dead in a hotel - and Jim suspects foul play. Drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul takes pity on an old family friend who is being victimised by the residents of a local housing estate because his dog attacked a neighbour's child. The situation rapidly explodes into full-scale war, and the doctor finds himself getting too closely involved in the conflict for comfort. Drama, with Nigel Le Vaillant and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon's husband Chris becomes seriously ill, leaving her facing a difficult decision. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph star in an episode featuring an early screen appearance by Doon Mackichan

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey's wife sends her new boyfriend to the camp to ask him for a divorce. When the professor is unwilling to co-operate, the pair begin digging for evidence. Classic comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Social climber Hyacinth indulges another of her flamboyant whims and decides she and Richard need a new car - but a modest three-door hatchback is not what she had in mind. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry buys Glenda a chaise longue from Auntie Wainwright's shop as an anniversary present, but it soon proves to be more than just something to sit on. Meanwhile, Howard finds himself locked in Pearl's bedroom with no means of escape, so Truly, Clegg and Billy set out on a rescue mission. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Mike Grady

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a woman is found dead at a health spa, a convalescing Dalziel decides to mix business with pleasure and books himself in - but finds more than he bargained for. Meanwhile, Pascoe is bitten by the love bug and runs the risk of making a real fool of himself. Norman Wisdom guest stars, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An unknown tagger begins to paint graffiti around London, and claims to have been responsible for killing a celebrated street artist four years previously. Originally part of a gang, the victim went solo after receiving support from a wealthy art patron, and when the detectives reopen the case, they become suspicious of his resentful former crew members. Meanwhile, Brian signs up to a social networking site as TopCop999 to chart the gritty life of a lawman, much to his colleagues' displeasure

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Two further bodies and a skeleton are found at the former boarding house and Walker isn't convinced that the alibi of former guest Graham Richards is enough

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: The Cold Case squad tries to solve a murder dating back to the Second World War. In 1948, a former conscientious objector was found killed with a nine-inch nail driven into his head. New evidence suggests he may have been the victim of a state-sponsored execution, and there may be other crimes to be uncovered. Feature-length episode, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Vic's first love turns up in Skelthwaite, filling Peggy with misgivings, and Stephen wallows in gloom because Jacqui appears immune to his charms - but Patricia's romantic prospects suddenly seem a good deal brighter

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton and Meadows play a psychological game, putting pressure on the teenage partygoers by staging a reconstruction and holding a press conference to rattle the composure of the suspects - aiming to uncover the truth about the death of the young athlete. Eddie finds a piece of wood which he believes is the murder weapon, but the nail that caused the puncture wound in the victim's spine is missing

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira struggles to cope with working alongside Greg and Rafi - and as the two men's friendship grows, her life at home deteriorates. Malick is delighted when Hanssen offers him the opportunity to become Clinical Skills Teacher, but is then unsure what to do when his boss makes a potentially dangerous request. Meanwhile, Eddi's brother Liam (Emmerdale's Luke Tittensor) turns up at Holby and pleads for her help

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A depressed Guppy is forced to make a tough decision on an emergency rescue and grows increasingly despondent when he makes a mistake that puts a patient's life in danger. Stitch calls the disillusioned medic to his office and places temptation in his path by revealing he uses cocaine to cope with the stress of work. Meanwhile, Charlie tries to persuade a bouncer to seek help after learning he is self-harming. Jodi Albert, Roy Hudd and Scott Mills guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of three. Human remains found buried in a park turn out to be those of a student who went missing 20 years previously. Suspicion immediately falls on her boyfriend and a couple they were close to in their teens. The case brings Heaton face to face with an old adversary, raising the possibility that the man he sent down for murder in the 1980s may in fact be a serial killer

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The inhabitants of Albert Square are horrified to hear what's happened to Billy, while Sarah and Joe have a shocking announcement for Lorraine and Irene

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Jacksons wait for news of Billy, and the police tap their phone

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah arrives in Paris, hoping to reconnect with her husband, and is shocked by what she finds. Elizabeth pushes George to be truthful, but causes more damage to their already fragile relationship. Regina puts the next stage of her plan into action, and while Anna finds out the truth about her real father, Olivia proves the depths she is prepared to go to in order to save her marriage

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim investigates a drug courier who had his package lifted by an elusive Parisian pickpocket, who is now trying to sell his ill-gotten gains. However, he repeatedly finds himself foiled by the cunning felon and later faces charges of brutality. Detective drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul comes into conflict with DI Dagley when an inmate he treated after a prison riot dies in police custody. The surgeon vows to get to the bottom of the case, unaware of the web of intrigue surrounding the wife of the dead man. Marty plays the good uncle as Alison goes into labour. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: In a bid to raise some cash for Christmas, Tracey falls back on her typing skills, while Sharon reckons there is a gap in the market for her own unique brand of pet care

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted faces the potentially awkward task of hiring an X-rated film for the artistically inclined male campers. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to share her unparalleled experience of being the perfect hostess by advertising her talents in the local newspaper. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom attempts to step into his father's shoes by riding Compo's bicycle and, enthused by Clegg and Truly's anecdotes, he sets about performing wonders of his own on the two-wheeled bone-shaker. Meanwhile, Pearl is delighted when Howard gets stuck on the roof, enabling her to keep an eye on him, and Glenda is dismayed to learn Barry has challenged a member of the office staff to a duel - refereed by Billy

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Suspicions are aroused when the holiest house in Kembleford is linked to the death of a wanton woman. Drama, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Catherine arrives home with Ilinka, an emaciated Croatian woman who has been rescued after being trafficked and enslaved for four years, and later arrests a lad at a high school who has been caught dealing drugs. Meanwhile John's panic over Vicky's threat spills out into his home life. Crime drama, starring Sarah Lancashire and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: When a retrospective exhibition of murdered fashion designer Ritchie Levene's work opens, the team is prompted to re-examine the three-year-old crime. The detectives learn about his tempestuous relationships with his new spouse, personal assistant and ex-wife Sarah, who believes his brother Adrian is the killer because he had the most to gain from his death. Meanwhile, Gerry enlists Emily to help update his image, although not everyone is impressed with the results. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Hannah Waterman

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Back on the murder squad, Pat is asked to investigate when a young mother of two, Diane Leach, is abducted from a local wood. The confession of a local sex offender complicates matters, as Pat looks into the possibility that the father of a young protege of the woman may be responsible for her disappearance. Meanwhile, Walker has worries of his own, as his ex-wife Lyn is dating an abusive, unstable man, who turns into a manic-obsessive when she decides to leave him

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: An abandoned car belonging to a former Home Secretary is discovered in a Belfast garage, containing a skeleton and an unexploded bomb. Although the garage owner, missing since 1981, may have had links to the IRA, a more disturbing theory soon emerges - that this was the work of British secret service assassins, carefully staged to look like a terrorist outrage. Feature-length episode, guest starring Timothy West, Frances de la Tour and Christopher Strauli

programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring more second-round matches. There was heartbreak for British number one Kyle Edmund at this stage two years ago when he was beaten in five sets by experienced Spaniard Fernando Verdasco. That match was followed on Centre Court by a comfortable 6-3, 6-2, 6-2 victory for eventual champion Novak Djokovic over Denis Kudla

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Cameras visit a one-bedroom flat in Thornton Heath, south London, a piece of land in Ash in Kent and a semi-detached house in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton. All of the associated projects of their new owners are ambitious, but promise to deliver stunning results.

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the case of a servant, accused of murdering his upper-class benefactor in Devon in 1884, who made history as 'The Man They Could Not Hang'

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper guide two teams scouring Ballinderry in Northern Ireland for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Anita Manning pays a visit to Lisburn Cathedral to find out more about a daring feat of a local suffragette

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fourth day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, as the second round continues. Rafael Nadal came through a tough test against Nick Kyrgios at this stage two years ago, prevailing, 6-3, 3-6, 7-6, 7-6, while there were victories for three other highly decorated players on the No. 1 Court, with Petra Kvitova, Roger Federer and Serena Williams defeating Kristina Mladenovic, Jay Clarke and Kaja Juvan respectively. Elsewhere, Ashleigh Barty demonstrated why she was top seed with a 6-1, 6-3 win against Alison Van Uytvanck

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: On the morning of Linda's birthday, she receives an unexpected present from Max - and is even more surprised to notice the postmark. Frankie throws her stepmum a party, where Rocky and Shirley are getting on like a house on fire, although he is smitten when Kathy shows up. Ruby tells Jean she believes she doesn't deserve happiness

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie, Stuart and Bernie head to their insemination appointment - with Violet tagging along - after which Bernie receives her first instalment, but it's less than she was expecting. Phil tells a stunned Kat that he wants Raymond to know he is his father, while Nancy suggests to Linda that it's time Shirley and Frankie knew about the parentage of her baby. Chelsea still has her eyes set on Gray, asking Denise to babysit for his kids while they go out for a meal

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An inventor looks for investment in his quirky pet products and a pair of optometrists hope the Dragons will see a bright future in their anti-blue light filtering idea. A schoolteacher-come-food entrepreneur tempts the Dragons' taste buds as well as their pockets with her low-sugar children's jams and an eco-entrepreneur pitches a carbon-offsetting trading app. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian house in London's Crystal Palace, a chalet bungalow in Rackheath near Norwich, and a terrace house in Canterbury

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a complex case from Victorian Britain that featured four prime suspects, two trials, and ultimately just one man hanged for the murder of his neighbour. In 1882, 43-year old shop-keeper Harriet Candler was killed in a brutal attack, with her property ransacked

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp back at some gadgetry buys of the past, dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts. Anita Manning gets to learn all about the speaking clock, while Christina Trevanion gets the lowdown on barometer restoration

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fifth day from the All England Club, as the third round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. At this stage two years ago, eventual champion Novak Djokovic was made to work hard at first before eventually finding his groove and prevailing 7-5, 6-7, 6-1, 6-4 against Hubert Hurkacz, while Karolina Pliskova was a 6-3, 2-6, 6-4 victor over Hsieh Su-wei

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Former winners Miles Jupp, Zoe Lyons, Neil Hannon and Lucy Porter are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of David Gower, Jacques Cousteau, Merchant Ivory's EM Forster film adaptations and Victoria Wood

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Belgium v Italy (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the second quarter-final, which takes place at Fußball Arena München. Belgium eliminated holders Portugal in the previous round thanks to a fine strike by Thorgan Hazard and a solid defensive display to withstand heavy pressure in the second half, and they will now be looking to banish memories of their shock defeat at the hands of Wales at this stage of the 2016 tournament. The Italians were also made to work hard in the last 16, with extra-time goals from substitutes Federico Chiesa and Matteo Pessina helping the Azzurri to the win over Austria, despite them conceding their first goal in 12 matches shortly after through Sasa Kalajdzic's header

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: As the quarter-finals get underway, Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: MOTD Top 10: Euros All Time XI
MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the opening two quarter-finals from either Saint Petersburg Stadium or Fußball Arena München

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate a lethal shooting from 1937 in which a barge captain was allegedly murdered by his best friend

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Norfolk's biggest Antique Centre in King's Lynn. Experts Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey assist the Reds and the Blues as they do battle

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis steps in for Peter Jones, joining regular Dragons Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies to assess more money-making schemes. A musical inventor shows off his drumming training device, and four children make a first aid demonstration to secure investment for their parents' business. Plus, pitches for culinary alcohol and a prototype shoe mechanism

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the third day from the All England Club, featuring second-round matches. Eventual women's champion Simona Halep prevailed 6-3, 4-6, 6-2 against Romanian compatriot Mihaela Buzarnescu at this stage two years ago, and then 15-year-old prodigy Coco Gauff impressed again with a 6-3, 6-3 victory over Magdalena Rybarikova. There was British disappointment as Heather Watson lost in straight sets to 20th seed Anett Kontaveit, and Wimbledon remained the only Grand Slam to elude Stan Wawrinka when he lost to Reilly Opelka in five sets. Other notable victors on day three in 2019 included Karolina Pliskova, Caroline Wozniacki, Kevin Anderson and Milos Raonic, who defeated Monica Puig, Veronika Kudermetova, Janko Tipsarevic and Robin Hasse respectively

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the third day's play from the All England Club, where the men's and ladies' singles were due to continue with round two, as the players continued their bids to win the titles claimed by Novak Djokovic and Simona Halep when the tournament was last held in 2019

programme.title: Stonehenge: The Lost Circle Revealed
programme.desc: Using cutting-edge research, a dedicated team of archaeologists has compiled the evidence to fill in a 400-year gap in man's knowledge of the bluestones of Stonehenge. By combining innovative 3D scanning techniques, traditional field archaeology and novel laboratory analysis, the team has discovered where the stones were quarried and where they first stood. Professor Alice Roberts shows how the team made its discoveries and joins Professor Mike Parker Pearson to piece together the final parts of the puzzle, solving one of the toughest challenges ever faced by archaeologists

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: John McEnroe: Still Rockin' at 60
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents an intimate profile of the tennis champion. The programme features contributions from family and friends including his wife - new wave singer-songwriter Patty Smyth - long-time sporting rival Bjorn Borg, and Chrissie Hynde of the Pretenders, who provides an insight into McEnroe's musical passions. Sue pays a visit to McEnroe's art gallery and examines how he has continued playing until the age of 60 after his retirement from the professional circuit

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson, Brian Cunningham and George Anderson focus on people gardening in smaller spaces. Kirsty plants up a range of trendy containers with plants to attract pollinators, while George is growing quick crops in small spaces

programme.title: Inside Culture
programme.desc: Mary Beard hands over the reins to cultural historian Shahidha Bari to explore the importance of self-expression, from the clothes we wear to the art and music we make. Shahidha visits artist Tracey Emin in her home town of Margate, and asks if is there a cost to revealing so much in your art. In Dundee, Shahidha also visits the new V&A exhibition on nightclub design

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan investigate how British hedgehogs are coping this spring, and Megan McCubbin joins them live in Norfolk to discuss efforts to save the corncrake. Iolo Williams is in the Scottish Highlands, checking on some very special wildcats, and Gillian Burke has a fishy tale about flapper skates in Northern Ireland

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Nick Hall provide advice to two teams competing at Nottingham Racecourse. An exhilarating auction sees the two teams make profits galore. Presented by Charlie Ross

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development potential of a Warwickshire property above a former brewery, a Staffordshire house with a huge back garden and a former farm building in Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Will Foster compete to remodel a couple's 1950s home in Belfast, with Lei Ying dreaming of a perfect kitchen, while her husband David hopes for a cosy home and a safe route to the garden. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fourth day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, as the second round continues. There were mixed fortunes for British players on the corresponding day when the tournament was last held in 2019, with Cameron Norrie and Jay Clarke losing to Kei Nishikori and Roger Federer respectively, but Johanna Konta and Dan Evans booking their places in the third round with wins against Katerina Siniakova and Nikoloz Basilashvili. Andy Murray also delighted the home support when he and doubles partner Pierre-Hughes Herbert won their first-round match against Marius Copil and Ugo Humbert in four sets. Elsewhere, 2018 champion Angelique Kerber's reign came to an end when she was beaten 2-6, 6-2, 6-1 by lucky loser Lauren Davis, and fan favourite Marcos Baghdatis played his final match at SW19 when he lost 6-1, 7-6, 6-3 to Matteo Berrettini

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the fourth day's play at the All England Club, where the men's and ladies' second-round singles were scheduled to conclude. Places in the last 32 were at stake with matches in the top half of the ladies' draw and the bottom half of the men's, as the quest to succeed Novak Djokovic and Simona Halep as champions continued

programme.title: Being Frank: The Frank Gardner Story
programme.desc: The BBC security correspondent explores what it is like to suddenly become disabled, something he experienced himself 16 years ago after being shot by Al-Qaeda gunmen in Saudi Arabia. The bullets damaged his spinal nerves, leaving him partially paralysed and he has used a wheelchair ever since. In this deeply personal film, he talks candidly about the effects his injuries have had on his life, work, relationships and the way he views himself. And he speaks to others, to explore how they have responded to sudden life-changing injuries

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Action from the second contest in the three-match series, held at The Oval

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. First up they have to make little girls' bridesmaid dresses, complete with puff sleeves, lined bodices and big bows. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct evening gowns worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers Ben and Myles are forced to call the police when they are confronted by a hostile crowd at a used car dealership in Yorkshire that sold a defective vehicle

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood lifts the lid on what really happens to Britain's waste, finding out how non-recyclable rubbish is turned into energy to power homes and businesses

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the case of a servant, accused of murdering his upper-class benefactor in Devon in 1884, who made history as 'The Man They Could Not Hang'

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper guide two teams scouring Ballinderry in Northern Ireland for bargains to take to auction. Plus, presenter Anita Manning pays a visit to Lisburn Cathedral to find out more about a daring feat of a local suffragette

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan round up this year's nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to the Moray Firth to check on dolphins, and Gillian Burke encounters basking sharks in Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fifth day from the All England Club, as the third round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Eventual champion Simona Halep produced a strong display at this stage two years ago to defeat former world number one Victoria Azarenka 6-3, 6-1, but it was 15-year-old Coco Gauff who continued to take many of the headlines when she overcame Polona Hercog 3-6, 7-6, 7-5. In the men's tournament, there was a minor upset when fourth seed Kevin Anderson lost to Guido Pella in straight sets, and David Goffin won one of the best matches of the day in five sets against Daniil Medvedev

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the fifth day's play at the All England Club, which featured matches in the top half of the men's draw and bottom half of the ladies' draw. The players will have been eager to complete their fixtures today, which would give them a full weekend off to prepare for the last 16 on Monday, and a break from the intensity of the third Grand Slam tournament of the year

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost adds tall plants for shady conditions to his borders, shows how to make a window box for growing vegetables and herbs and gives tips on how to grow plants successfully in peat-free compost. Nick Bailey visits Kew Gardens to explore their collection of ferns and cameras return to the urban garden of Kate Bradbury to find out how she ensures that visiting wildlife is kept well fed throughout the summer months. Plus, the Isle of Man garden where a range of exotic plants are thriving in the microclimate on the island and a nurseryman in Kent with a passion for hydrangeas

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Borg vs McEnroe (2017)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1980, acclaimed Swedish tennis player Bjorn Borg is training for an unprecedented fifth consecutive Wimbledon men's singles title, with only one thing standing in his way - the brash and abrasive young American John McEnroe. A rivalry develops between the pair, culminating in one of the greatest matches of all time in that year's final. Biopic, starring Sverrir Gudnason, Shia LaBeouf and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Ranvir Singh, Micky Flanagan, Sophie Willan and Reginald D Hunter to discuss Shuggie Bain by Douglas Stuart, and The Frequency of Us by Keith Stuart, and they also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a peaceful landscape, with distant foothills and velvety shrubs surrounding the quiet waters of an inlet

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Chronicle of life on board the luxurious Galaxy cruise ship. Singer Jane visits a medium, casino workers Dale and Mary celebrate their wedding anniversary by having a go at rollerblading and Jack steps into the shoes of injured lead dancer Philip - will he learn the routine in time?

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Using clips from the science strand's archive, this film examines how people have learnt to live with new emerging technology of the time, and how artificial intelligence systems power our daily lives

programme.title: The Joy of AI
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili looks at how man has created machines that can simulate, augment, and even outperform the human mind - and why this shouldn't cause alarm. He reveals the story of the pursuit of AI, the emergence of machine learning and the recent breakthroughs brought about by artificial neural networks. He shows how AI is not only changing the world, but also challenging man's very ideas of intelligence and consciousness. Along the way, the programme investigates spam filters, meets a cutting-edge chatbot, looks at why a few altered pixels makes a computer think it's looking at a trombone rather than a dog

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A sensitive and moving account of a year in the life of Sasha, a seven-year-old French girl assigned male at birth, and her family's fight for her acceptance. The film follows her parents as they come to terms with their daughter's gender dysmorphia and reveals how Sasha is affected by the societal norms that make it far from easy for her to experience childhood in the same way as most of her peers

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. They include a rare collection of the first openly gay magazine - featuring the rising young singer David Bowie - to letters from worried parents of newly 'out' daughters and sons. Presented by Stephen K Amos and Susan Calman

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The second of two documentaries in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. Focusing on the past 30 years, it features the nun-impersonating freedom-fighting Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence, the writer behind a famous lesbian kiss and a Muslim man who set up an LGBTQ support group for South East Asians. Presented by Susan Calman and Stephen K Amos

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a peaceful landscape, with distant foothills and velvety shrubs surrounding the quiet waters of an inlet

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Chronicle of life on board the luxurious Galaxy cruise ship. Singer Jane visits a medium, casino workers Dale and Mary celebrate their wedding anniversary by having a go at rollerblading and Jack steps into the shoes of injured lead dancer Philip - will he learn the routine in time?

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia struggles to abide by the rules and clashes with Mrs Lamb as she attends her first-ever school sports day, where apparently it's no longer acceptable to cheer for individual children and spectators can only offer generic support like 'good job'. Over at the Kidiverse soft-play centre, Kevin is devastated that an important work presentation means he will miss the dads' race. Liz worries that her new boyfriend is blanking her, and Anne discovers something that leads to a nasty surprise for Amanda

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jack and Michelle have an encounter with a school of Mexican dolphins, while casino workers Dale and Mary swap the roulette wheel for rollerblades, sending everyone running for cover

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of Ernest Hemingway, considered one of the greatest American writers. The documentary examines Hemingway's enduring influence on literature and culture and also penetrates the myths surrounding him to reveal a deeply troubled, controversial and ultimately tragic figure. The story begins with his childhood in Illinois, his time with the Red Cross during the First World War, his marriage and his move to Paris, where he started work as a writer

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Krasner's pledge to end the execution of prisoners is put to the test after a police officer is murdered while trying to protect citizens during a robbery

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jack and Michelle have an encounter with a school of Mexican dolphins, while casino workers Dale and Mary swap the roulette wheel for rollerblades, sending everyone running for cover

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A selection of archive footage from the arrival of the so-called beat boom in 1964, which catered to the art-school craze for rhythm and blues. Featuring performances by Tom Jones, the Kinks, Manfred Mann, the Rolling Stones, John Lee Hooker and the Pretty Things

programme.title: Island at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by artists associated with the Island record label during its 50 years. Tracks include Cat Stevens' Father and Son, Roxy Music's Do the Strand and Bob Marley and the Wailers' Stir It Up. The programme also features Steel Pulse, U2, PJ Harvey, Bob Marley, Amy Winehouse and Baaba Maal

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the edition first aired on March 28 1991, with performances by Dannii Minogue, Gary Clail On-U Sound System, the Bee Gees, Snap, Scritti Politti featuring Shabba Ranks, Definition of Sound, the Rolling Stones, Chesney Hawkes and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on April 4 1991, featuring performances by Inspiral Carpets, N-Joi, the Mock Turtles, Feargal Sharkey, Chesney Hawkes, the Simpsons, the Waterboys, Black Box and Mike & the Mechanics

programme.title: Canadian Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of performances by Canadian artists from the BBC archives

programme.title: BBC Four Sessions
programme.desc: Country singer kd lang performs at LSO St Luke's in London with a 30-strong string section from the BBC Concert Orchestra. The set features tracks from across her 25-year career, including Constant Craving, covers of Neil Young and Leonard Cohen songs and material from her most recent album Watershed

programme.title: Bublé at the BBC
programme.desc: Entertainment special in which crooner Michael Bublé performs a selection of his best-known hits as well as songs from his then album Nobody But Me, accompanied by his band and a 30-piece orchestra at MediaCityUK in Salford. In between numbers he also talks to Claudia Winkleman about his career, marriage, fatherhood and more

programme.title: In Concert: Gordon Lightfoot
programme.desc: A concert by the Canadian singer-songwriter, accompanied by Red Shea and Richard Haynes. The trio perform songs including Summer Side of Life, Saturday Clothes, That's What You Get for Loving Me, If I Could Read Your Mind and Steel Rail Blues

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the edition first aired on March 28 1991, with performances by Dannii Minogue, Gary Clail On-U Sound System, the Bee Gees, Snap, Scritti Politti featuring Shabba Ranks, Definition of Sound, the Rolling Stones, Chesney Hawkes and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on April 4 1991, featuring performances by Inspiral Carpets, N-Joi, the Mock Turtles, Feargal Sharkey, Chesney Hawkes, the Simpsons, the Waterboys, Black Box and Mike & the Mechanics

programme.title: Canadian Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of performances by Canadian artists from the BBC archives

programme.title: Little Big Awesome
programme.desc: Children's animation Gluko and Lennon, two inseparable buddies who get up to all sorts of crazy adventures in their home of Townopolis

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: There's relief all around when Penfold's pilfering of Squawk's gadgets and clandestine late night trips to shady warehouses turns out to preparation for his taking part in 'bot battles' - a fight club for homemade robots. With the help of an engineer and her special cameras, Maddie show us how the gears and weights are controlled by a computer. Next, it's time for lift-off as Maddie heads to a space museum. We see how a spacesuit is made from lots of different layers of special materials to keep astronauts safe when they are working in space, and Maddie uses her special thermal camera to see how the suit keeps astronauts warm.

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the photo of Bob and Ted and remembers the day she got it. When Bob’s identical twin, Ted, arrives at the Big Hub for a visit, Molly and Mack can’t believe how alike they are. Bob and Ted tell them how they used to pretend to be one another when they were boys. This gives Molly an idea! Bob and Ted could play the same trick again, on everyone in the Hub. Soon, Molly and Bob are up on the balcony watching Ted pretending to be Bob at his stall. At first, the plan goes well, but things soon start to go wrong. Ted gets everyone’s names mixed up and he doesn’t know his plums from his apricots. Finally, when Moira asks ‘Bob’ to fix her spinny chair, we discover that Ted is a bit useless at mending things, and the game is most definitely up!

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Love Monster and Elder Kitten lead the Fluffytowners in singing a funky, tidying up song as they each pick up a piece of litter to clean the green. Everyone takes their piece of litter to the recycling centre where they queue up to do their bit for the environment.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is relaxing at home, playing games and watching television. She shows us how a television screen works using millions of tiny squares called pixels. Using her special microscope camera, Maddie shows us how a pixel can make any colour from just three coloured lights - red, blue and green. Next she visits a factory where carpet is made. Maddie follows the journey that wool takes, from shearing a sheep to going through the factory, to end up in the carpets we have at home. Maddie uses her special slow-motion camera to show us how the different coloured threads are woven together on a loom to make a carpet.
