programme.desc: Icklewoo is feeling really left out, so at playtime Mrs Grizzlesniff suggests she sits on the Buddy Bench so the other monsters will see she is lonely and come and play with her

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The sleepwalking Snowy Snooze is so fast asleep that it walks straight into B.O.T.! The sleepy beastie shuffles onwards through the snow and B.O.T. tries to keep out of its way whilst still collecting the data for a successful mission.

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day reveals the different ways in which animals sleep, revealing how many young apes must learn how to make a bed out of leaves. In Tibet, bar-headed geese make a soft fluffy nest out of feathers on the hard rock, while Meerkats spend the night in burrows underground

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to draw some pictures using charcoal.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a horse puzzle and a macro safari, and watch as Book Boy makes a merry-go-round.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid steps through the magic curtain to become a shelf-stacker in a supermarket. He sets about his tasks enthusiastically and needs his wits about him to negotiate the perils

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Gurdy is having lots of little ideas, who run all over the tree. There's no room for the Olobobs! So they create Pie Piper, who enchants the ideas and gives them a yummy pie

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grandmaster Glitch builds his effigy in ice, his digging threatens the Lambert Glacier in Antarctica

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back three million years where he tracks down the ancient tribe of australopithecus primates, but he has his trusty backpack stolen by hungry vultures

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's Mum, Jess has just married Doug's Dad, Simon. The new family get home to discover a lost pup hiding in the wedding presents. Evie wants to keep him and call him Waffle

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Emily does not know what to do with a delivery of umbrellas and Jack tells the story of a special shipment he once received

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Timmy shows Tig how to draw monsters, but she gets a fright when she puts the pictures under her bed and hears them roaring in the night

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘treasure’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We find lots of different treasure – gold treasure, royal treasure, sunken treasure, jungle treasure and even yummy treat treasure! Dee and a friend have a go at juggling treasure, and we see Dee hide treasure. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Letters double up in the tub and go slip sliding down a hill. L discovers that bath-time is more fun when its doubled up

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Twenty-One reveals her six-step plan for figuring out Numberblocks - and takes some friends along for the ride. Learn different ways to explore the properties of a number with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog Loves Art Pug wants to learn how to paint, so our heroes visit Picasso's studio in Paris.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Smelly socks and cheesy feet are brie-lliant, thinks Messy, but when it is Lofty's feet that smell, it is a giant problem

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour pink, from marshmallows to flamingos, as well as explore the colour found in nature around the world. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour pink.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends step back in time, and Mr Tumble has some clock trouble.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: On a cold day in Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies find big snowballs, dance with a snowman and watch children make a caterpillar from snow

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing tries Coco's hula hoop, but it is harder than it looks and he ends up breaking the hoop

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch's giant ball game threatens El Castillo, so the Go Jetters attempt to save it

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep paint portraits of one another, but argue about who has the bigger brush

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: International footballer Rachel Yankey and her team learn how to be right on target by practicing their aim. Meanwhile, it's a windy day at Pupton Park, and the Footy Pups have to come up with a plan to retrieve the footballs

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace looks back at some of her favourite machines that are electric, fast and useful

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook is laughing so much he almost falls overboard, so Captain Captain insists they learn what to do if one of them actually did fall overboard

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is upset when a leaf falls from a tree, and Roly finds an acorn stuck to his bottom, prompting the squirrels to search for more to share out

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter promises the Shrew some worms to entice him to Cotton-tail's birthday party, he ends up accidentally putting everyone in danger. Adventures with the animal and his friends, in a tale inspired by Beatrix Potter

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The potatoes sing a punk-rock anthem about the wonders of being themselves

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: CBeebies presenter Andy is jetting off all over the world and meeting amazing animals living in and nearby water. Get ready to explore oceans, rivers and lakes

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate heads outdoors, finding out about things that use the wind. She finds out how a kite works when it flies high in the sky, shows how to measure the wind speed, and uses a slow-motion camera to explain how a kite catches the wind to stay in the air. Next, Maddie finds out how electricity is made on a wind farm. She visits a wind turbine to see how it turns wind energy into electricity.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: A trip to the local creek opens Bluey’s eyes to the simple beauty of nature.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch and his grimbots have a long race along the Great Wall of China

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack is reminded of his mum's guitar, whilst Moira finds some roller boots in the Hub

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gramps teaches Waffle to read while Mrs Hobbs is dog-sitting Jelly next door

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo can't find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The little girl and her feathery friend have a sleepover with some friends, but struggle to get to nod off

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle sneezes while skipping through the garden, prompting his friends to do the same

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Frances Barber reads A House in the Woods, by Inga Moore

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a quirky property in Fulham, a huge renovation in Tamworth, Staffordshire, and a terrace in Bradford

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring the opening second-round action from the outside courts

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring more second-round matches. There was heartbreak for British number one Kyle Edmund at this stage two years ago when he was beaten in five sets by experienced Spaniard Fernando Verdasco. That match was followed on Centre Court by a comfortable 6-3, 6-2, 6-2 victory for eventual champion Novak Djokovic over Denis Kudla

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out on a quest to discover the truth about rugby. It's a story of vodka, Groggs and adventures away, full of lively characters from the nation's sporting past

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Cameras visit a one-bedroom flat in Thornton Heath, south London, a piece of land in Ash in Kent and a semi-detached house in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton. All of the associated projects of their new owners are ambitious, but promise to deliver stunning results.

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the case of a servant, accused of murdering his upper-class benefactor in Devon in 1884, who made history as 'The Man They Could Not Hang'

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper guide two teams scouring Ballinderry in Northern Ireland for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Anita Manning pays a visit to Lisburn Cathedral to find out more about a daring feat of a local suffragette

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fourth day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, as the second round continues. Rafael Nadal came through a tough test against Nick Kyrgios at this stage two years ago, prevailing, 6-3, 3-6, 7-6, 7-6, while there were victories for three other highly decorated players on the No. 1 Court, with Petra Kvitova, Roger Federer and Serena Williams defeating Kristina Mladenovic, Jay Clarke and Kaja Juvan respectively. Elsewhere, Ashleigh Barty demonstrated why she was top seed with a 6-1, 6-3 win against Alison Van Uytvanck

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: On the morning of Linda's birthday, she receives an unexpected present from Max - and is even more surprised to notice the postmark. Frankie throws her stepmum a party, where Rocky and Shirley are getting on like a house on fire, although he is smitten when Kathy shows up. Ruby tells Jean she believes she doesn't deserve happiness

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie, Stuart and Bernie head to their insemination appointment - with Violet tagging along - after which Bernie receives her first instalment, but it's less than she was expecting. Phil tells a stunned Kat that he wants Raymond to know he is his father, while Nancy suggests to Linda that it's time Shirley and Frankie knew about the parentage of her baby. Chelsea still has her eyes set on Gray, asking Denise to babysit for his kids while they go out for a meal

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An inventor looks for investment in his quirky pet products and a pair of optometrists hope the Dragons will see a bright future in their anti-blue light filtering idea. A schoolteacher-come-food entrepreneur tempts the Dragons' taste buds as well as their pockets with her low-sugar children's jams and an eco-entrepreneur pitches a carbon-offsetting trading app. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian house in London's Crystal Palace, a chalet bungalow in Rackheath near Norwich, and a terrace house in Canterbury

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a complex case from Victorian Britain that featured four prime suspects, two trials, and ultimately just one man hanged for the murder of his neighbour. In 1882, 43-year old shop-keeper Harriet Candler was killed in a brutal attack, with her property ransacked

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp back at some gadgetry buys of the past, dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts. Anita Manning gets to learn all about the speaking clock, while Christina Trevanion gets the lowdown on barometer restoration

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fifth day from the All England Club, as the third round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. At this stage two years ago, eventual champion Novak Djokovic was made to work hard at first before eventually finding his groove and prevailing 7-5, 6-7, 6-1, 6-4 against Hubert Hurkacz, while Karolina Pliskova was a 6-3, 2-6, 6-4 victor over Hsieh Su-wei

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Former winners Miles Jupp, Zoe Lyons, Neil Hannon and Lucy Porter are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of David Gower, Jacques Cousteau, Merchant Ivory's EM Forster film adaptations and Victoria Wood

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Belgium v Italy (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the second quarter-final, which takes place at Fußball Arena München. Belgium eliminated holders Portugal in the previous round thanks to a fine strike by Thorgan Hazard and a solid defensive display to withstand heavy pressure in the second half, and they will now be looking to banish memories of their shock defeat at the hands of Wales at this stage of the 2016 tournament. The Italians were also made to work hard in the last 16, with extra-time goals from substitutes Federico Chiesa and Matteo Pessina helping the Azzurri to the win over Austria, despite them conceding their first goal in 12 matches shortly after through Sasa Kalajdzic's header

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: As the quarter-finals get underway, Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: MOTD Top 10: Euros All Time XI
MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the opening two quarter-finals from either Saint Petersburg Stadium or Fußball Arena München

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring more second-round matches. There was heartbreak for British number one Kyle Edmund at this stage two years ago when he was beaten in five sets by experienced Spaniard Fernando Verdasco. That match was followed on Centre Court by a comfortable 6-3, 6-2, 6-2 victory for eventual champion Novak Djokovic over Denis Kudla

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. Here they bid for an unusual item of hairdressing memorabilia, something salvaged from a tip, a classic piece of industrial furniture and a beloved toy

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Ulster to the Arctic: A Gentleman's Adventure
programme.desc: In June 1856, The 1st Marquess of Dufferin and Ava, Frederick Hamilton-Temple-Blackwood, left his home in Bangor to travel across Scotland and on to the Arctic in a small sailing schooner. After travelling 6,000 miles in four months, he published an account of his journey and became the most outstanding diplomat of the Victorian era. Now adventurer and travel writer Leon McCarron recreates this seminal voyage by following the accounts written in Lord Dufferin's book, introducing viewers to some of his hero's stories

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Cameras visit a one-bedroom flat in Thornton Heath, south London, a piece of land in Ash in Kent and a semi-detached house in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton. All of the associated projects of their new owners are ambitious, but promise to deliver stunning results.

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the case of a servant, accused of murdering his upper-class benefactor in Devon in 1884, who made history as 'The Man They Could Not Hang'

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper guide two teams scouring Ballinderry in Northern Ireland for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Anita Manning pays a visit to Lisburn Cathedral to find out more about a daring feat of a local suffragette

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fourth day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, as the second round continues. Rafael Nadal came through a tough test against Nick Kyrgios at this stage two years ago, prevailing, 6-3, 3-6, 7-6, 7-6, while there were victories for three other highly decorated players on the No. 1 Court, with Petra Kvitova, Roger Federer and Serena Williams defeating Kristina Mladenovic, Jay Clarke and Kaja Juvan respectively. Elsewhere, Ashleigh Barty demonstrated why she was top seed with a 6-1, 6-3 win against Alison Van Uytvanck

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: On the morning of Linda's birthday, she receives an unexpected present from Max - and is even more surprised to notice the postmark. Frankie throws her stepmum a party, where Rocky and Shirley are getting on like a house on fire, although he is smitten when Kathy shows up. Ruby tells Jean she believes she doesn't deserve happiness

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie, Stuart and Bernie head to their insemination appointment - with Violet tagging along - after which Bernie receives her first instalment, but it's less than she was expecting. Phil tells a stunned Kat that he wants Raymond to know he is his father, while Nancy suggests to Linda that it's time Shirley and Frankie knew about the parentage of her baby. Chelsea still has her eyes set on Gray, asking Denise to babysit for his kids while they go out for a meal

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: James Acaster, Dani Dyer, Oti Mabuse and Fred Sirieix join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An inventor looks for investment in his quirky pet products and a pair of optometrists hope the Dragons will see a bright future in their anti-blue light filtering idea. A schoolteacher-come-food entrepreneur tempts the Dragons' taste buds as well as their pockets with her low-sugar children's jams and an eco-entrepreneur pitches a carbon-offsetting trading app. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: The Irish Open - Highlights
programme.desc: Stephen Watson presents action from day one at Mount Juliet Estate in Thomastown, Kilkenny, as the tournament won by America's John Catlin last year got under way. There was a three-way tie at the end of this round last year in Ballymena, with Englishmen Aaron Rai and Jordan Smith and South Africa's Dean Burmester all carding five-under-par rounds of 65. With analysis by Des Smyth

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian house in London's Crystal Palace, a chalet bungalow in Rackheath near Norwich, and a terrace house in Canterbury

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a complex case from Victorian Britain that featured four prime suspects, two trials, and ultimately just one man hanged for the murder of his neighbour. In 1882, 43-year old shop-keeper Harriet Candler was killed in a brutal attack, with her property ransacked

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp back at some gadgetry buys of the past, dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts. Anita Manning gets to learn all about the speaking clock, while Christina Trevanion gets the lowdown on barometer restoration

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the fifth day from the All England Club, as the third round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. At this stage two years ago, eventual champion Novak Djokovic was made to work hard at first before eventually finding his groove and prevailing 7-5, 6-7, 6-1, 6-4 against Hubert Hurkacz, while Karolina Pliskova was a 6-3, 2-6, 6-4 victor over Hsieh Su-wei

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Former winners Miles Jupp, Zoe Lyons, Neil Hannon and Lucy Porter are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of David Gower, Jacques Cousteau, Merchant Ivory's EM Forster film adaptations and Victoria Wood

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Belgium v Italy (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the second quarter-final, which takes place at Fußball Arena München. Belgium eliminated holders Portugal in the previous round thanks to a fine strike by Thorgan Hazard and a solid defensive display to withstand heavy pressure in the second half, and they will now be looking to banish memories of their shock defeat at the hands of Wales at this stage of the 2016 tournament. The Italians were also made to work hard in the last 16, with extra-time goals from substitutes Federico Chiesa and Matteo Pessina helping the Azzurri to the win over Austria, despite them conceding their first goal in 12 matches shortly after through Sasa Kalajdzic's header

programme.title: The Irish Open - Highlights
programme.desc: Stephen Watson presents action from day two at Mount Juliet Estate in Thomastown, Kilkenny, as the tournament won by America's John Catlin last year continued. Australia's Maverick Antcliff carded the best score of round two last year in Ballymena with an excellent five-under 65 to leave him with a share of the lead at the halfway stage alongside England's Aaron Rai. With analysis from Des Smyth

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: As the quarter-finals get underway, Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: MOTD Top 10: Euros All Time XI
MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the opening two quarter-finals from either Saint Petersburg Stadium or Fußball Arena München

programme.title: Bing

programme.desc: Science is brought to life with fun experiments that can be tried at home, including cleaning a rusty bike with cola, and reading secret messages with X-ray vision

programme.title: Kerry is Kirsty
programme.desc: Sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee start their latest cycling route north of Tyndrum at the foot of Beinn Dorain before continuing along Loch Lyon passing under the West Highland railway line. They meet hill walker Colin MacLeod, whose bike is a means to an end for reaching more summits

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about uses of water, including ice baths, cliff jumping and divination

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Following a group of individuals from all over the country, bringing a unique insight through detailed personal accounts of the beginning of 2021, when the government announced that Scotland would go back into lockdown

programme.title: Rocky Ros Muc
programme.desc: Documentary charting Sean Mannion's life and boxing career, examining his spirit inside the ring and his troubles outside from Ros Muc in Co Galway to Boston in America

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon ditches the car and makes his way on foot, following the Fife Coastal Path, between the pretty seaside villages of Earlsferry and Pittenweem

programme.title: Leasan sa Bhas
programme.desc: A story of addiction, lust and murder on Scarp in the late 19th century, examining whether it is fact or island folklore

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: The autumn chill is turning the forest gold when King Sigurd takes Gudrun to gather nuts for a feast. However, after Gudrun falls asleep beside a tree she awakes to find her basket empty. She's not the only one gathering nuts for the winter

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig is asked to make an unusual delivery to the school as the pupils prepare for their play - a special yeti suit

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Magaidh's new prize pig has gone missing, and Shane is challenges to make a salad that Mario will eat

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: Ela tries to piece together Ben's cryptic clues as she goes in search of a mobile phone

programme.title: Su Pic (Peek Zoo)
programme.desc: It’s very cold outside but it is vital that it is warm and toasty inside, as Crogaidh is expecting her chick to hatch any time soon

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Daibhidh Walker reads Hamish agus an Trèana, written and illustrated by Daisy Hirst, and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Penfold is jealous of Danger Mouse's growing collection of medals, which leads him to fall under the spell of Quark's latest money-making scheme

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia is chased when she’s out, and she comes across something that she is not expecting

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Tha tòrr plòigh a’ tachairt ‘s iad a’ putadh na cloiche an-diugh. There’s lots of fun happening as they shot-put today.

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda has the power to sing and say everything in rhyme, as if it were a musical. Charlie makes a video of her singing with his cell phone

programme.title: An t-Eilean (From Skye with Love)
programme.desc: the first calf sale of the year is held in Portree, Fiona competes at a power lifting event, and students visit an interesting croft in Staffin

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)

programme.title: An Là (News)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: From his island cottage in Callanish, Gregor MacLeod guides viewers through delicious recipes from traditional Scottish baking to exciting new combinations

programme.title: Bruadar a' Bhàis (A Dream of Death)
programme.desc: When a young man was convicted of murder in Inverness in 1830, it was the only time in Scots legal history that testimony from second sight was admitted in court. This dramatised documentary revisits the crime and subsequent trial

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads head out of the kitchen to Harris to visit Roddy's father for home-grown potatoes, and his cousin for cockles to make chowder. Uisdean sources seafood for a Jambalaya

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: In Ethopia, as children wake up to the first rays of sunshine the temperature is already over 30 degrees Celsius. They live in the Danakil desert in the north-east of the country, near an active volcano, in a region that is the world's hottest on average. Getting to school is quite an ordeal, especially if they encounter sandstorms

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum Maclean heads to Applecross in Wester Ross, travelling from the edge of the sea to the top of hills to catch that special photo

programme.title: Treubh an Tuathanais
programme.desc: When Coinneach the cockerel decides that he is the best person to be the farm leader, Mona challenges him and, as a result of this, they put it to a vote

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Chasing his wooly ball, Baba finds himself in a seagull's nest on the cliff edge. However, a storm is raging and heading for the rock, putting the little pufflings in danger

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig is asked to make an unusual delivery to the school as the pupils prepare for their play - a special yeti suit

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo?discovers little seeds in an apple. From each seed a plant can grow, and each plant can turn into a tree

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: Miguel's life becomes full of bananas - and he's keen to keep track of every single one

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Wilma Kennedy reads Bilidh agus a’ Bhiast, written and illustrated by Nadia Shireen and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Following the activities of a four-day-old reindeer that lives with its family in Ivvavik National Park, Canada. Although well protected from the cold, finding food is the main challenge facing the animal, which must rely on its nose to sniff out lichen during the winter

programme.title: A' Chuil
programme.desc: Choirstaidh is back with another programme from her studio A’ Chùil. She makes a sign, and other things, out of string to decorate her studio

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Tossing the hammer is the latest activity

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Instead of continuing with his hibernation, Mùmantrobha is drawn into taking part in a winter sports competition with athlete Monseigneur Brisg

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Catriona Murray talks about how poetry can evoke memories of a person. She reads “An Luchag ‘s an Cat” which immediately reminds her of her cousin who is no longer alive

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on the early days of famous sports topics

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod explores the language and landscape of the Cuillin, a range of rocky mountains on the Isle of Skye. Beginning his journey at Bruach na Frithe with local mountain rescuer Ewen MacKinnon, he then meets Allan Turner on a walk from Coruisk to Glen Sligachan, who explains the history of the area

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Sweeny questions his future as a full time crofter. As he gets organised for an essential day trip to Uist, he shares his thoughts on how he feels about making the journey

programme.title: Is Mise an Teanga
programme.desc: Documentary about the Gaelic language, with contributions by contemporary poets from the Outer Hebrides, Dublin, Connemara, West Belfast and Glasgow

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Featuring award-winning Scots singer Iona Fyfe, accompanied by multi-instrumentalist Graeme Rorie and guitarist Jack McRobbie

programme.title: Eilbheas
programme.desc: Drama set on the island of Lewis on the day of Elvis Presley's death in 1977. A man's life is transformed by an unexpected encounter with the undisputed king of rock 'n' roll. Starring Greg Hemphill and Sean MacLeod

programme.title: Cuirm @ Celtic
programme.desc: Duncan Chisholm and the Scottish Ensemble perform at the Kelvingrove Museum and Art Gallery as a part of Celtic Connections

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean makes his way to Glen Brittle on the Isle of Skye to discover a waterfall called Eas Mòr, which is located at the foot of the Cuillin and offers a wonderful landscape

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Bridlington, Yorkshire, where Alys Dobbie is dazzled by some emeralds, and Tim Hogarth is unsure if he has stumbled upon a masterpiece

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five nightclub workers from Bridgwater take on the Tenable Tower and attempt to win a £125,000 jackpot. Can they score a perfect 10 and bring the money home?

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Kathryn, Richard, Sophie and Larry pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard spots there is something wrong with Faith. Jacob is suspicious of Meena and confronts her. The Vivienne opens Emmerdale Pride

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David pesters Gail to allow Nick to see Sam. James attracts unwanted online attention prior to a press conference. Fiz offers to support Tyrone

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the lush Ionian island of Corfu, beginning in its capital, Corfu Town, where she discovers a surprising, cosmopolitan city more like a little slice of Italy than Greece. After sampling the delights of its famous mumquat flavoured ice-cream, a cookery lesson in making signature savoury dish pastitsada reveals the island's centuries-old love affair with exotic spices. Julia meets a pair of brothers passionate about revolutionising production of Corfu's oldest and most important export - olive oil- and ends her stay trying out the secret to staying young that all Corfiots swear by

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Family feuding causes heartache for Gail. James bravely goes public with his sexuality. Tyrone avoids telling Fiz of Alina's pregnancy

programme.title: Extraordinary Twins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following Nick and Chelsea from Idaho, whose twin daughters Callie and Carter are joined together at the sternum. The programme meets the family as the twins approach their fourth birthday, and their parents face the agonising decision of whether to put them through complex surgery to separate them. To help them make this fateful decision, the family heads on an emotional journey to meet other conjoined twins and their parents. Narrated by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: On Assignment
programme.desc: Rageh Omaar's monthly series continues with Ben Chapman in Spain's popular holiday destination, Gran Canaria, where the pandemic has left many tourism businesses close to collapse. There, he finds many are also worried about the impact of the continuing influx of migrants arriving by boat from Africa - both on the authorities' ability to cope and on the island's image once the holidaymakers return. And he meets a hotelier couple who have recently taken in a large group of asylum seekers

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Terminal 4 turns blue, airside officer Hugh has a potential environmental mess on his hands, and an octogenarian forces herself into the country under the watchful eye of Passenger Experience Guru Demi. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith gives the wrong idea. Vic and David bond over their babies. Ben takes a mysterious and unwelcome phone call

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.desc: The actor learns how to look after vulnerable creatures as he travels the world. Martin heads to the Ningaloo reef in Australia and swims with the world's biggest fish, the whale shark, before taking part in the oldest Royal tradition - swan upping - on the River Thames

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.desc: Henry Cole rides through the south of France, where he finds some of the twistiest roads imaginable. Back home, he meets the designers behind Aston Martin's first ever bike and tells the story of the Suzuki 750, the bike nicknamed 'the Kettle'

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Switzerland v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the first quarter-final at Saint Petersburg Stadium, as both sides face each other after progressing in two thrilling round-of-16 clashes that both needed extra time. Spain threw away a 3-1 lead with just five minutes to go in their previous fixture against Croatia, before finally winning the game 5-3 thanks to two quick goals from Alvaro Morata and Mike Oyarzabal. The Swiss beat favourites France 5-4 on penalties after also coming back from 3-1 down in their last-16 tie

programme.desc: Oxford plays host to a veteran film star, in town to visit a local cinema. programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Switzerland v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the first quarter-final at Saint Petersburg Stadium, as both sides face each other after progressing in two thrilling round-of-16 clashes that both needed extra time. Spain threw away a 3-1 lead with just five minutes to go in their previous fixture against Croatia, before finally winning the game 5-3 thanks to two quick goals from Alvaro Morata and Mike Oyarzabal. The Swiss beat favourites France 5-4 on penalties after also coming back from 3-1 down in their last-16 tie

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is pestered by the sister of Aidensfield's landed gentry, while Greengrass has his sights set in the same direction - poaching on the lord's land

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry begins his job helping Chris and it is not long before Chris is driving him round the bend. Tricia and Lyn both have the hots for Paddy's cousin Jason

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice asks Tricia to be her bridesmaid. Claudia rebuffs Chris's advances. Ashley decides to stage a panto. Paddy is confused as to why Jason is flirting with Tricia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve asks Maxine not to tell Fiona about what happened. Sally is frustrated by the time it is taking for her to get the girls back. Curly receives a visit from the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is charged with trying to extort money from Freshco. Steve accuses Maxine of wrecking Fiona's life. Emily and Spider worry about Curly when he disappears

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Two grieving widows lay rival claims to a corpse found washed up on the beach, each insisting it is the body of her missing husband. While it is obvious only one can be telling the truth, the detective finds it tricky to discern which of the women is lying - and why. Drama, guest starring Eleanor Bron, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: A Canadian officer is brought to Downton for recuperation and disrupts everything. Ethel hears distressing news, Bates makes another attempt to settle matters with Vera, while Carson receives a tempting offer. Matthew gets used to life in a wheelchair with Mary as his round-the-clock carer. Costume drama, starring Amy Nuttall, Brendan Coyle, Jim Carter, Dan Stevens and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Albert unveils ambitious plans for an international exhibition - but has to find a way to fund it. Lord Palmerston causes uproar when he sends the navy in to blockade Greece after an attack on a British subject, and Monmouth hires Penge to spy on Sophie and Joseph - which leads to him discovering revealing information about Joseph's time at Chatsworth

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The world's eyes are on the Great Exhibition - and the Royal couple. Does triumph or failure beckon? Lives will be changed for ever as intrigue unfolds amongst the exhibits. Drama, charting the early years of the reign of Queen Victoria, witnessing her transformation from the flighty Princess Alexandrina into the formidable monarch, mother and queen who would preside over the construction of an empire. Period drama, starring Jenna Coleman, Tom Hughes, John Sessions, Laurence Fox and Nicholas Audsley

programme.desc: Bernice asks Tricia to be her bridesmaid. Claudia rebuffs Chris's advances. Ashley decides to stage a panto. Paddy is confused as to why Jason is flirting with Tricia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is pestered by the sister of Aidensfield's landed gentry, while Greengrass has his sights set in the same direction - poaching on the lord's land

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates the link between trespassers on the local railway line and thefts from a fur factory, culminating in a dramatic chase on board a moving train. Meanwhile, Greengrass acquires a metal detector and thinks he has struck gold - but someone else gets to it first. Nick Berry and Bill Maynard star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy receives confirmation that Elsa is now Alice's legal guardian. Graham consoles her and invites her out for dinner. Ashley has concerns about Bernice and Gavin's relationship

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned wants Roy and Kelly to spend Christmas with him in Ibiza. Emily is worried that her dad will spend Christmas alone and wants to invite him to the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve tells Fiona she was right to sack Maxine. Emily, Spider and Alma want to help Curly, and Kevin struggles when the child minder falls ill

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine insists that Fiona is told the truth about her and Steve. When the police search for Curly, Emily Spider and Alma cover for him. Sally begs Kevin to let her see the girls

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of a sheep farmer leads Wycliffe into the heart of a rural community whose apparently tranquil surface conceals a desperate struggle for survival. It is not long before he uncovers disturbing evidence suggesting foul play. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the residents try to return to normal life following the armistice, a new development stuns them. Meanwhile, Carlisle puts Anna in a difficult position, Bates reels from the news about Vera, and Thomas embarks on a new money-making scheme. Costume drama, starring Iain Glen, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Rob James-Collier

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The unorthodox but brilliant North East detective investigates when charred human remains are found in an abattoir furnace, and attempts to deduce how and why they got there in the first place. The bones are identified as belonging to a fellow cop, leading Vera to uncover a web of dark secrets as she looks into her dead colleague's fraud squad. Brenda Blethyn dons her familiar hat and mac to play Vera in this mystery, based on the characters created by Ann Cleeves

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shrewd Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale

programme.title: The Danish Girl (2015)
programme.desc: Artist Gerda Wegener unveils a portrait of her husband Einar dressed as a woman in 1920s Copenhagen. Einar realises that the woman in the painting is his true identity, and decides to become one of the first people to undergo sex reassignment surgery. Period drama based on David Ebershoff's novel inspired by the true story of artist Lili Elbe, starring Eddie Redmayne and an Oscar-winning Alicia Vikander

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local gamekeeper is injured in a shooting incident on the moors and suspicion falls on his disgruntled predecessor, whose 11-year-old daughter provides damaging testimony. Elsewhere, Maggie discovers her ex-husband has started work at the hospital and Greengrass schemes away as usual, risking Gina's displeasure by persuading Steve to install a pool table at the pub. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is heading for a fall, with neither Turner nor Terry willing to tolerate being ordered around by him. Ashley cancels the pantomime. Butch invites John round for Christmas

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the day of the Christmas Show, but Ashley has no star act. Zak's latest money-making scheme does not go to plan. Marc disrupts Sean and Angie's plans for a family Christmas

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally feels she cannot cope without the girls. Vera and Jack are furious when Alec docks their rent from their wages. Jim urges Fiona not to cast Steve away over just one mistake

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie helps Kevin out by looking after Sophie. Jim accuses Fiona of lying to him to get back at her for wrecking her wedding. She tells him Steve really did try to kill him

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The wedding approaches and excitement is in the air, but Branson's plans could rattle everyone. Spanish flu reaches the house and a desperate Thomas looks for a way to re-establish himself. Ethel faces a dilemma when the Bryants return with a heartbreaking proposition, and a decisive Anna forces Bates to think about their future. Costume drama, starring Allen Leech, Rob James-Collier, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Brendan Coyle

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Rita is amazed to hear Sally has moved in with Greg. Steve takes Fiona out for the afternoon. Toyah's cousin Kirsty invites her to go on holiday to San Antonio

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona apologises to Steve for being so selfish. Jim has pains in his leg and hopes that means it is healing. Sally signs the partnership contract without reading it

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Diane invites both Alan and Eric to hers for dinner, but both are unaware the other has been invited. Kelly pleads with Chris to let her and Roy have the cottage

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice is keen for Diane to leave. Paddy's cousin Jason arrives in the village. Diane questions Bernice's decision to marry Gavin

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is alerted when a lay preacher is wounded in a shooting accident. Meanwhile, Greengrass unearths the prized recipe for the famous Aidensfield buns

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's desire to play the guitar proves too much for his flatmates. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is pestered by the sister of Aidensfield's landed gentry, while Greengrass has his sights set in the same direction - poaching on the lord's land

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry begins his job helping Chris and it is not long before Chris is driving him round the bend. Tricia and Lyn both have the hots for Paddy's cousin Jason

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice asks Tricia to be her bridesmaid. Claudia rebuffs Chris's advances. Ashley decides to stage a panto. Paddy is confused as to why Jason is flirting with Tricia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve asks Maxine not to tell Fiona about what happened. Sally is frustrated by the time it is taking for her to get the girls back. Curly receives a visit from the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is charged with trying to extort money from Freshco. Steve accuses Maxine of wrecking Fiona's life. Emily and Spider worry about Curly when he disappears

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Two grieving widows lay rival claims to a corpse found washed up on the beach, each insisting it is the body of her missing husband. While it is obvious only one can be telling the truth, the detective finds it tricky to discern which of the women is lying - and why. Drama, guest starring Eleanor Bron, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: A Canadian officer is brought to Downton for recuperation and disrupts everything. Ethel hears distressing news, Bates makes another attempt to settle matters with Vera, while Carson receives a tempting offer. Matthew gets used to life in a wheelchair with Mary as his round-the-clock carer. Costume drama, starring Amy Nuttall, Brendan Coyle, Jim Carter, Dan Stevens and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Albert unveils ambitious plans for an international exhibition - but has to find a way to fund it. Lord Palmerston causes uproar when he sends the navy in to blockade Greece after an attack on a British subject, and Monmouth hires Penge to spy on Sophie and Joseph - which leads to him discovering revealing information about Joseph's time at Chatsworth

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The world's eyes are on the Great Exhibition - and the Royal couple. Does triumph or failure beckon? Lives will be changed for ever as intrigue unfolds amongst the exhibits. Drama, charting the early years of the reign of Queen Victoria, witnessing her transformation from the flighty Princess Alexandrina into the formidable monarch, mother and queen who would preside over the construction of an empire. Period drama, starring Jenna Coleman, Tom Hughes, John Sessions, Laurence Fox and Nicholas Audsley

programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans it's an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Private investigator Caroline Selby seeks Arthur's help in locating eccentric recluse and inventor Albert Goddard, who has come into an inheritance. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar saves the life of the prime minister of a British colony. Guest starring Edward Woodward

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When the Ministry is broken into by an apparently invisible man, it seems a secret formula has fallen into the hands of an organisation run by Soviet Intelligence. Light-hearted action, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at memorable stagings of the race from Epsom Downs. Featuring brilliant performances from Shergar to Sea The Stars, plus interviews with personalities from the worlds of sport and entertainment

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage five of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 27.2km individual time-trial from Changé to Laval Espace Mayenne. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage six of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 161km route from Tours to Chateauroux. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Beyond the Amazon, in the remote jungles of Guyana, fishermen are disappearing without trace. Locals blame the 'water mama', a mermaid that is said to drag victims to their death. Could the largely unexplored rivers of this region be hiding a new monster, or is there a more familiar predator lurking beneath the surface? When an eyewitness claims to have seen the creature in a lake, Jeremy Wade's hunt for this mysterious bodysnatcher takes an unexpected turn, bringing him face to face with an astonishing beast

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole tours Majorca on a classic bike, begins the restoration of a vintage Harley-Davidson, and visits Bike Shed in London, the home of the New Wave custom scene

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage six of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 161km route from Tours to Chateauroux. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The first leg of The Tour of France sees Ali, Dovey and Spooner head to the south of the country near Marseille, to a large natural lake called Carces. This tricky wild water, situated within rocky terrain, is rumoured by locals to hold several carp over the magical 70lb mark. This adventure is far from plain sailing as the team are faced with some huge physical and mental challenges - extreme weather conditions, heavy weed beds and giant catfish.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits Papua New Guinea, an island renowned for cannibalism, macabre rituals, headhunters and bitter tribal conflict. However, a new threat is sweeping through the region and he sets out on the trail of a creature that has reportedly been tearing chunks out of fishermen and devouring male body parts

programme.title: Die Hard with a Vengeance (1995)
programme.desc: New York cop John McClane returns to duty following an explosion at a department store in the city. The perpetrator contacts the police and tells them that unless McClane and an unfortunate bystander who was in the wrong place at the wrong time carry out a series of instructions to the letter, more bombs will be detonated. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Samuel L Jackson and Jeremy Irons

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a foreign student, an apparent cocaine trafficker, is shot dead, Cowley tasks Bodie and Doyle with targetting teenage traffickers who have diplomatic ties with South America. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins, Gordon Jackson and Alice Krige

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits New Zealand in search of a mysterious 8ft creature as described more than 200 years ago by a famous explorer. In trying to discover whether he is dealing simply with a legend or a real flesh-and-blood monster that has allegedly attacked divers and even seized a child, he finds the remote rivers and deep lakes would provide an ideal refuge for the predator

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Dave's relief manager lets him down at short notice, he asks Terry to step into the breach and run the Winchester Club for him.
