programme.desc: Simon Templar tries to stop a crime ring forming a deadly alliance with psychology students who plan murders. Drama, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Ms Peel investigate the case of several businessman who have been killed by birds of prey - and discover a connection to a comic-book character

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. After foiling two assassination attempts, they soon appreciate others share information on his whereabouts. Charles Dance and Geoffrey Palmer feature alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes involved with a gambler, whose sudden death leaves the dodgy car dealer £2,000 worse off. Meanwhile, Terry helps his old flame Sylvie leave her abusive husband. Billy Connolly guest stars in the vintage comedy drama, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar rescues a Russian damsel in distress and agrees to help her fend off art thieves eager to get their hands on her da Vinci masterpieces. Cult adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage six of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 161km route from Tours to Chateauroux. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage seven of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 249.5km route from Vierzon to Le Creusot. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A selection of the best World Cup Goals through the years - beginning with Geoff Hurst's hat-trick for England in 1966, right up to the 2018 World Cup in Russia

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A report from Southeast Asia, of something in the water that's sliced cleanly through a young man's testicles, is enough to lure 'fishing detective' Jeremy Wade to the shores of the Mekong - a river long renowned for its monstrous fish. But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims who've been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade returns to Africa's Okavango Delta to reinvestigate the tigerfish - a pack-hunting carnivore previously blamed for mauling the victims of a capsized boat. If these fish are as lethal as Jeremy concluded, then why aren't there more reports of fatal, piranha-like attacks? In a bid to flush out the true nature of these sight-hunting predators, Jeremy puts on his dive gear and offers himself as live bait

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove continue their rebuild of a Harley WLC. Henry also visits the Norton factory at Donington and finds out about the iconic Sportser bike

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage seven of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 249.5km route from Vierzon to Le Creusot. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Border Force officer Rob deals with cargo trying to enter the country involving frozen chicken, and there is a surprise in store for passengers flying to Heathrow's top European destination

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Police officers are called in to deal with an emergency at one of the airport gates. Meanwhile, Ian shows off his muscles out on the airfield

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade has hunted down freshwater killers for 30 years, but only now has he discovered he was sharing those waters with darker, deadlier creatures - parasites.

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.desc: The team responds to a farm SOS, when a skid loader slides into a dam, as well as an extreme helicopter salvage mission that requires access to a protected nature reserve

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A notorious hard-nosed villain offers to co-operate with the police and gives himself up in return for protection for his wife and their children, so Bodie and Doyle are assigned to perform the task. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.desc: Part one of two. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth steer a 72-foot vessel along the waterways of Staffordshire, journeying along the Caldon Canal and the Trent and Mersey Canal, to Stoke-on-Trent. They begin their journey at Cheddleton Flint Mill, built in 1253, hearing why flint was such an essential pottery ingredient. programme.title: Inside Tesco: 24/7
programme.desc: The retailer's transformation from a cut-price upstart to market leader. After the death of its founder Jack Cohen in 1979, the company was in the middle of a high-risk transition and an outside takeover was a real danger. Ian MacLaurin, Jack Cohen's first management trainee, was chief executive at the time. His vision for the company was very different from Jack's pile-it-high, sell-it cheap approach. He set out to transform the company and compete with the number one retailer Sainsbury's - something that was achieved in 1995

programme.desc: The hotelier stays at the feted Marbella Beach Club, where she meets the man who has played host to A-list jetsetters for decades and has many interesting tales to tell. Moving on to the wild frontier terrain of Western Andalucia, an area often overlooked by tourists, Alex arrives in the quintessentially Spanish town of Jerez, the home of sherry, and learns how this rich drink is produced while admiring the amazing dancing steps of the local horses. Finally, she arrives in the vibrant city of Seville, capital of Andalucia, which she explores on bicycle

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Writer and historian Dan Jones examines the creation of the picturesque Kennet and Avon Canal, which was born from a murky pool of dirty deals and demanding landowners. It came into being thanks to the genius of young engineer John Rennie, whose inspiration and innovations created a waterway that linked Bristol to London. Dan discovers how Rennie plotted the canal's route using the most basic of surveying instruments and marvels at the engineering innovations that made it all possible, such as the water-powered pumping station at Claverton

programme.title: Kensington Palace: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Part two of two. A study of Queen Victoria's teenage years at Kensington Palace, and Princess Diana's wardrobe, including the dress she wore to dance with John Travolta at the White House. Plus, a look at the biggest renovation Kensington has seen in years, as chandeliers are reinstalled in the King's Drawing Room for the first time in more than a century

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Week By the Sea
programme.desc: The comedian visits Southend-on-Sea in Essex, home to the world's largest pleasure pier and the famous narrow-gauge railway. Susan also creates her own ice cream sundae, takes part in a training exercise with a local lifeboat crew, and talks to Birds of a Feather star Linda Robson about why she has been coming to the town since she was a child

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Cornwall to help Sean and Janine, the owners of Northshore, Bude's only hostel. Having returned from travelling the world, the pair bought the property to keep their travelling dream alive. Running the hostel is the best job Sean's ever had - but a shocking downturn in occupancy and their unorthodox living scenario have left Janine wanting out. Desperate to sort the business, the couple call on Alex's advice in a last-ditch attempt to resolve matters. The expert arrives with a few surprises up her sleeve, but quickly learns saving this business isn't going to be easy

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

Meanwhile, in Darwen, letting agent Paul issues proceedings on a tenant who is more than £3,000 in arrears, but Paul's attempts at evicting him are delayed when the tenant refuses to leave without Paul giving him a good reference

She then heads south to the small but picturesque town of Consuegra, whose windmills inspired some of the anecdotes in Don Quixote by Miguel de Cervantes. programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas presents a whistle-stop tour of awkward, embarrassing and cringeworthy holiday moments, as related by a selection of comedians and celebrities. Johnny's famous contributors skewer the traditional staycation, questioning the perks of rain-drenched British beaches, and whether anyone really likes going camping. Turning their attention further afield, the stars share some of their most memorable holiday tales, including transforming a hotel corridor into one long 'slip-and-slide', the suspicious discovery of a pair of chicken fillets in a swimming pool in Marbella, and a blush-inducing incident involving a naked paragliding session along a Spanish beachfront

programme.desc: Summer has finally arrived in Scotland, bringing long days and an abundance of food. programme.desc: The comedian concludes her coastal tour with a trip to Blackpool, where she enjoys a game of bingo, and is reunited with her Strictly Come Dancing partner Kevin Clifton

programme.title: Great Paintings of the World with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: This episode examines the story behind an iconic image created by arguably the most famous and influential artist of the 20th century, Pablo Picasso. Andrew Marr tells the harrowing and uplifting tale of his 'barbed-wire face-mask' of a painting, Weeping Woman

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Gabrielle, Blur's Alex James, Boyzone's Shane Lynch, Steps' Lee Latchford-Evans, and Hannah Spearritt from S Club 7 share their memories of pop stardom in 1996. Shaun Ryder, Tito Jackson, Terry Christian, Michaela Strachan, Rhona Cameron, Rowland Rivron, Toby Anstis and other famous faces guide viiewers through the biggest hits of the year. The Spice Girls hit the big time, Oasis were still riding high in the charts, and Babylon Zoo had a memorable smash with Spaceman, but who had the biggest-selling single of the year?

programme.title: 13 Moments...
programme.desc: An extraordinary musician with an unforgettable voice, Amy Winehouse's meteoric rise saw her blossom into a truly global star who changed the landscape of modern pop culture. However, the world also witnessed her drug and alcohol addiction, dramatic weight loss and involvement in a destructive relationship. These factors led to her premature death in July 2011, aged 27. This film features contributions from some of those who knew Amy well and those who tried to help her, telling the story of a towering but troubled talent, through the chain of key moments that led ultimately towards tragedy

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Spectacular Spain with Alex Polizzi
programme.desc: The presenter discovers a lush, mountainous region in the north-west with a very different climate and culture from the rest of Spain. In many ways Galicia resembles Ireland rather than southern Europe and after visiting its rugged stretch of shoreline notorious for its many shipwrecks, Alex meets Mari Carmen, who scrapes the prized local delicacy of gooseneck barnacles off the rocks to sell. Alex also explores the ancient forests of Ponferrada and Galicia's Celtic past, and makes her way to the pilgrim city of Santiago de Compostela, where she visits a martial arts school

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Ritz ranks high among London's most legendary hotels, one that has been synonymous with luxury and wealth, as well as being a bastion of privilege for over 100 years. Situated on Piccadilly, just a stone's throw from Buckingham Palace, it has been a home from home for the royal family since the reign of Edward VII. The Ritz has redefined service in luxury hotels, introducing the now-universal concept of the customer always being right. This programme examines its heritage, its famous afternoon teas and the scandalous history of its founder, Cesar Ritz

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice helps a widower sell his 200-year-old cottage so he can pack up and start a new life in Scotland. The property is basically sound, but has an air of bachelor neglect made worse by the owner's pet Labrador, which acts like it owns the place

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Estate agent Ann Maurice offers her expertise to a couple whose home in Rothwell near Leeds is still for sale after 10 months, despite having attracted more than 30 viewings. Her advice includes ridding the house of the all-pervading pet smell, replacing the threadbare stair carpet and adding a lick of paint in a bid to persuade the next viewer to make an offer

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Thomas is determined to keep the animals warm when the boiler at the animal park breaks down on a cold snowy day

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: When searching for elusive rumble top tigers, the Turbots fall into a cave deep inside a volcano and the Paw Patrol performs a paratrooper-style rescue from Skye's ultimate copter. Animated action adventure following a squad of six heroic puppies and their tech-savvy 10-year-old leader as they protect the community of Adventure Bay

programme.title: Waterloo Road (1944)
programme.desc: A soldier serving in the Second World War discovers his wife has betrayed him by having an affair with a petty crook, so he resolves to settle matters and deserts from the army. He returns to a Blitz-ravaged London to confront his rival, but his quest for retribution is interrupted by an air raid. Drama, starring John Mills, Joy Shelton, Alastair Sim and Stewart Granger

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1979)
programme.desc: An American heiress befriends an elderly lady on a continental train journey, but wakes one morning to find she has disappeared and the other passengers deny having ever seen the missing woman - leading to suspicions that a conspiracy is afoot. Remake of Hitchcock's 1938 mystery, starring Elliott Gould, Cybill Shepherd, Angela Lansbury, Herbert Lom, Arthur Lowe and Ian Carmichael

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Beneath the Planet of the Apes (1969)
programme.desc: A second astronaut arrives on the strange simian-dominated world searching for his predecessor. As he journeys into the infamous forbidden zone, he discovers an underground cult of psychic mutants. Meanwhile, a military coup has taken place among the ape society, leading to an inevitable battle. Sci-fi sequel, with James Franciscus, Charlton Heston and Kim Hunter

programme.title: Gods of Egypt (2016)
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian god is driven into exile when his evil brother murders their father and seizes his throne. He enlists the aid of a human thief in restoring justice, and in return offers to help the mortal to bring his murdered lover back to life. Fantasy adventure, starring Nikolaj Coster-Waldau, Brenton Thwaites, Gerard Butler and Chadwick Boseman

programme.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014)
programme.desc: A former soldier injured in the line of duty takes up a new career as a financial analyst for the CIA. However, his deskbound position changes to a dangerous undercover assignment when he discovers a plot to destroy the US economy. Spy thriller inspired by Tom Clancy's novels, starring Chris Pine, Keira Knightley, Kevin Costner and Kenneth Branagh, who also directed

programme.title: The Handmaiden (2016)
programme.desc: A conman posing as a count hires a petty criminal to help him marry a Japanese heiress by becoming her handmaiden and persuading her to accept his proposal. Psychological thriller based on Sarah Waters' bestselling novel Fingersmith, starring Kim Min-hee, Kim Tae-ri, Ha Jung-woo and Cho Jin-woong. In Korean and English

programme.title: Beautiful Thing (1996)
programme.desc: A tentative relationship develops between two very different gay teenagers - Jamie, a shy outsider living with his brassy single mother, and Ste, who is popular at school but regularly on the receiving end of violence from his father and brother at home. Coming-of-age drama, starring Glen Berry, Scott Neal, Linda Henry, Meera Syal and Ben Daniels

programme.title: When Eight Bells Toll (1971)
programme.desc: A Navy intelligence agent heads to Scotland, where he poses as a marine biologist to investigate the theft of millions of pounds in gold bullion from ships off the British coast. However, the locals counter his enquiries with hostility, especially when he sets his sights on a Greek tycoon, whom he suspects may be operating as a modern-day pirate. Thriller, with Anthony Hopkins, Robert Morley and Jack Hawkins. Based on a novel by Alistair MacLean

programme.title: Destroyer (1943)
programme.desc: A former welder working on the Navy destroyer whose construction he oversaw angers his crewmates with his relentless quest for perfection. Wartime adventure, starring Edward G Robinson, Glenn Ford, Marguerite Chapman and Regis Toomey

programme.title: Tomahawk (1951)
programme.desc: Frontier scout Jim Bridger doesn't approve when a gold rush leads the US army to build a fort on land occupied by the Sioux. The situation goes from bad to worse when a sadistic officer launches an unprovoked attack, prompting a vengeful tribe to go on the offensive and leaving Bridger the only one who can save the soldiers at the fort from certain death. Western, starring Van Heflin, Yvonne de Carlo and Alex Nicol, and featuring an early appearance by Rock Hudson

programme.title: Run Silent, Run Deep (1958)
programme.desc: A commander aboard a US Navy submarine has a titanic clash of egos with his second in command as the senior officer's obsession with sinking as many enemy vessels as possible, whatever the risk, spirals out of control. Second World War drama, starring Clark Gable, Burt Lancaster, Jack Warden, Brad Dexter and Don Rickles

programme.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The civilisation of Panem is consumed by a bloody civil war. Reluctant rebel leader Katniss Everdeen leads a band of her allies on a mission to end the conflict at one stroke by assassinating the despotic President Snow - but faces many dangers as she closes in on her target. Conclusion of the sci-fi adventure series based on Suzanne Collins' novels, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Donald Sutherland and Philip Seymour Hoffman in his final role

programme.title: The Equalizer (2014)
programme.desc: A retired secret agent applies his talents to the task of helping victims of injustice. When he witnesses a prostitute being beaten up by a pimp, he kills the man, an act that draws him into a battle to the death with a Russian crime syndicate. Action thriller based on the 1980s TV series, starring Denzel Washington and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Hear My Song (1991)
programme.desc: The owner of a struggling nightclub claims he will host a performance by the legendary reclusive tenor Josef Locke. He ends up booking an imposter, but when the ruse is exposed, he faces the wrath of both his girlfriend and the entire community, so heads to Ireland to redeem himself by persuading the real Locke to come out of retirement for one last show. Fictional comedy based on a real-life incident, starring Adrian Dunbar and Ned Beatty

programme.title: Stratton (2017)
programme.desc: A British Special Boat Service commando tracks down an international terrorist cell. Thriller, starring Dominic Cooper, Austin Stowell and Gemma Chan

programme.title: The Long Memory (1952)
programme.desc: A man is released from prison after serving a 12-year sentence for a crime he did not commit, and immediately sets about tracking down three witnesses who lied in court, bringing about his conviction. However, while seeking revenge, he discovers the case is more complicated than expected. Thriller, starring John Mills, Elizabeth Sellars, John McCallum and Geoffrey Keen

programme.title: Shenandoah (1965)
programme.desc: A Virginian farmer is left to raise seven children after his wife dies. He tries to keep his family out of the American Civil War, but when Union troops abduct his son, and his son-in-law enlists with the Confederates, he is unable to maintain his neutral stance and is dragged into the conflict. Western, starring James Stewart, Doug McClure, Rosemary Forsyth, Katharine Ross and Patrick Wayne

programme.title: Star Trek Special
programme.desc: Leonard Nimoy and Jonathan Frakes discuss their roles as both actors and directors in the long-running sci-fi series

programme.title: Hatari! (1962)
programme.desc: A group of reckless animal trappers travels through the wilds of Africa, but the arrival of a beautiful photographer looks set to cause upheaval among the group. Comedy adventure, starring John Wayne, Red Buttons, Hardy Kruger, Elsa Martinelli and Michele Girardon

programme.title: Star Trek: The Motion Picture (1979)
programme.desc: A mysterious, planet-sized energy cloud is heading for Earth, destroying everything in its path. Admiral James T Kirk returns to the bridge of the Enterprise to stop the invader, but he has to contend with a bitter rival and the possession of one of his crew by the alien force. Sci-fi adventure, the first movie based on the TV series, starring William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy, DeForest Kelley and Persis Khambatta

programme.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan (1982)
programme.desc: The Enterprise crew faces a bitter battle to the death with an old enemy. Khan, a genetically engineered superhuman tyrant, has escaped exile on a barren planet and hijacked a starship to take his revenge on Admiral Kirk, while also scheming to turn an experimental planet-creating device into a doomsday weapon. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy, Ricardo Montalban and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock (1984)
programme.desc: Admiral Kirk and his crew hijack the decommissioned Enterprise to recover the body of their fallen comrade Mr Spock from the planet Genesis. Kirk has learnt that Spock's spirit is still alive, and the Vulcan can be resurrected, but he must also battle a Klingon captain determined to control the planet's mysterious energy. Sci-fi sequel, directed by Leonard Nimoy, with William Shatner, DeForest Kelley and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home (1986)
programme.desc: The Enterprise crew travels back in time to prevent an alien probe destroying the Earth. The only thing that can avert the disaster is a humpback whale, a creature extinct in the 23rd century - but adjusting to life back in 1986 proves a distinct challenge. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring William Shatner, DeForest Kelley and Leonard Nimoy, who also directed

programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a restaurant in Mount Sinai, New York, which appears to be stuck in the 1980s. The crumbling decor and unenthusiastic head chef at the Handlebar need to be tackled, but owners Bill and Carolyn LeRoy are in denial about the problems they face

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Dr Shaw decides that Riley is the reason Danny cannot focus during ice hockey and bans her from his next match. Meanwhile, Riley worries things are moving too fast with Fitch, and Tucker competes with Jenna for a promotion at work. Comedy, starring Chelsea Kane

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben tries to get Emma into daycare by flirting with the assistant manager, Megan, who insists there is no space, until she thinks that Ben and Tucker are a gay couple. Meanwhile, Riley worries that Dr Amy Shaw is trying to change Danny when he turns his back on their traditional 'Halfaween' costume party. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau, and guest starring Grace Phipps and Lacey Chabert

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel asks Joe to act as a translator when she has a meeting with a Japanese businessman, but things turn a little awkward when they all get together for dinner. Meanwhile, Lennox and her nemesis are forced to work on a school project together. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Despite everything that has happened to him over the past year, Joe decides to go to his college reunion to see his former classmates. However, when his date for the event cancels on him, Mel feels she must try to find a solution to the problem. Meanwhile, Lennox and Ryder plan to throw a party. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

Can Boyle save the day?

programme.title: Baby Daddy

programme.desc: Dr Cox discovers a member of Lucy's study group cheated on a test, and decides to punish the entire class until the guilty party steps forward. programme.title: Scrubs

Medical comedy, starring John C McGinley and Donald Faison

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Captain Holt is taken by surprise when his long-time nemesis, Deputy Chief Madeline Wuntch (guest star Kyra Sedgwick), arrives to carry out an inspection of his precinct. Peralta and Jeffords test the boundaries between 'work friendship' and 'real friendship' while they investigate a stabbing at a trendy milk bar. Boyle searches for a guest to take to his ex-wife's engagement party - will Diaz come to the rescue?

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon isn't pleased by the newfound popularity of Stuart's comic book shop when an author puts the place on the map. When Raj attempts to take the credit for Penny's astronomical discovery, friendships are tested. Howard and Bernadette try to arrange a date night

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: t's the final night in Edinburgh, hosted by food and fashion-conscious Bruce. All week Bruce has expressed his desire for the finer things in life and his dislike of anything that falls below his high standards, so expectations are high. Can Bruce pull the evening off with style, and will he do enough to grab the cash?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. She is joined by 25-year-old estate agent Hollie-Anna from Margate and 29-year-old Jaison from Norwich, a transsexual man who feels like he has exhausted his local dating pool. They will both choose a date after seeing what their potential partners have to offer on a purely physical level. But will they prove to be a good match?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: The story of a family divided by agricultural hardship who ended up living on opposite sides of the world, and as the team races to find the heirs to a recently advertised unclaimed estate, a surprise connection is made in Malta

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners. Henry and Simon realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast iron fire guard and some old wooden workshop clamps

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim, Fuzz and the team must race against the clock when they step in and restore high end car, a 1967 Lancia Flavia coupe, that has been hidden in a garage in Rochdale for 30 years

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The owners of a Spanish kite school debate moving their business to Kenya, a power cut causes problems at a busy B&B and two French holiday hosts are snowed under with jobs to do

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Old Times Guesthouse in Ipswich, where mother-and-daughter duo Linda and Kelly Langdown hope a go-karting session will get the contest off to a flying start. However, a lack of breakfast options and corner-cutting on the guests' orders do not go down very well

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit the Flower Pot in Sunbury-on-Thames on their second night, where owner Simon Bailey and French manager Pierre Cudon provide stylish continental-inspired accommodation, but the hosts are left puzzled by some of the feedback they receive the next morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the family-run Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, which has been a life's work for hotelier Jo Knowles. On arrival, Pat and Nigel find a suspicious stain, before the guests put on their war faces for a game of paintball. Jo takes everyone to a floating restaurant for dinner, and the next morning she presents a huge spread for breakfast - but her diners are suspicious that it has been laid on specially for the competition

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants stay at the Mariners Beachside B&B in Seaton, Devon, but are disappointed by the cleanliness of the property. They take part in a town-crying competition during the afternoon, and host Nigel feels under pressure to deliver his award-winning breakfast the next morning. Come feedback time, the owners discover not all their guests share the same opinion

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid, and confront their rivals. Simon Bailey and Pierre Cudon from the Flower Pot feel the feedback left by Nigel and Pat Booth is a step too far, while the Mariners B&B owners hit back at the men's opinion of their establishment's cleanliness. Linda and Kelly Langdown of the Old Times Guest House question the rating of their facilities, while the Dolphin Hotel's Jo Knowles has a confession to make regarding her hosting

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Craig Jarvis's tiny 65-square-metre home overlooking Taylor's Mistake has been six years in the making - Grand Designs New Zealand's longest running house build. Chris Moller finds out whether it is finally finished

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and the team deal with two emergency cases in this edition. Lara the cat went missing after jumping into a removal van, but escaped, only to be hit by a car and then attacked by a fox. One of its rear legs is shattered and the vet has to perform reconstructive surgery to rebuild it. Chief, an eight-year-old Northern Inuit, dislocated its hip while chasing a fox. Noel thinks it needs hip-replacement surgery, but owner Nigel can't afford to pay for the operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a Japanese-Welsh fusion home in the Wye Valley, completely transformed from a damp old forester's house in 2013. Tamayo Hussey had missed Japan ever since she moved to the UK 15 years earlier with husband Nigel, so to stave off the homesickness they decided to turn the property into a one-of-a-kind home, complete with roof bath, tatami room and sliding paper walls. Kevin finds out how this somewhat improvised building weathered one of the wettest winters on record and if it has brought the calm and tranquillity Tamayo remembers from her childhood in the Far East

programme.title: Antiques Hunters on Tour
programme.desc: Documentary following antiques dealer and French polisher Rob Cain as he visits France in search of hidden treasures to restore and sell back home in Yorkshire

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and numbers-quiz, inviting Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan to take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. John Cooper Clarke joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Birmingham, paramedics Adi Ahmed and Mark McDonagh rush to a 10-year-old boy who has had a major epileptic fit. At the same time in Coventry, an elderly woman with heart and lung failure is struggling to breathe but refuses to go to hospital for treatment. Plus, in Warwick, a crew is called to an elderly man who has extreme anxiety and has called emergency services 14 times

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: Antiques Hunters on Tour
programme.desc: Documentary following antiques dealer and French polisher Rob Cain as he visits France in search of hidden treasures to restore and sell back home in Yorkshire

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to California to investigate alternative dairy products at one of California's leading producers of almond milk, before meeting a scientist who has been raising genetically engineered goats whose milk he hopes might one day save half a million lives every year. Jimmy Doherty teams up with a fish detective on a covert mission buying and testing fish in Edinburgh to make sure it is the real deal, while Matt Tebbutt finds out what goes into spreadable butter

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates a potentially devastating by-product of Greek yoghurt production

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers try to solve the tricky case of a man named Smith. Plus, the story of a man whose troubled childhood led him to become separated from his adoptive family, and details of more unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend travel to Essex, where they rescue a 1974 Chevrolet Corvette Stingray that has been off the road for years. However, they soon discover it may be a bigger job than they anticipated when they get the iconic US vehicle back to the workshop

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Guests check into an unfinished holiday home, a power cut leads to dinner in the dark for restaurant owners in France and a couple of newlyweds search for a new home in Spain

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Borgue Hotel in Dumfries and Galloway, which owners Vince and Shirley Barrett run with military precision. On arrival, guests Adam and Naomi are not impressed with the decor, while Ken and Elaine do not like the orange towels. Later, over dinner, the hosts reveal who wears the trousers in their relationship, before Ken's jokes go down like a lead balloon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Allscott Mill B&B in Telford, Shropshire, on their second night, where hosts Ken and Elaine Purchase welcome their guests. On arrival, Shirely and Vince find fault with the lack of headroom in their lodgings, while Adam and Naomi are bemused by the absence of a toilet brush in their bathroom

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Park House in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where owners Ian and Joan Barr hope their colourful B&B will impress their guests. Adam and Naomi are wowed by the vibrant decor, but Ken and Elaine take issue with a dirty toilet brush

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Naomi and Adam Black welcome the contestants to East Thorne yurt retreat in Bude, Cornwall. They are pleased to discover it is Vince and Shirley's dream location, but Ken and Elaine don't share the same opinion. Later, the guests take part in an archery competition, and in the morning, are unimpressed with the DIY breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival owners meet for the final time on the last day of the competition to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Hosting skills, breakfasts, facilities and two toilet brush gripes are aired before the cash is counted and the winner is announced

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Creating a 50-metre-long castle wall is just the first of many uphill battles a brave family faces as its members tackle the gargantuan task of creating a medieval castle in north Canterbury

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Two-year-old Labrador Ira is fitted with a bionic spine, while anxious owner Rob brings in eight-year-old French bulldog Tom, who has a long list of chronic ailments. Matt and Rebecca arrive at the clinic with their three-year-old cat Marley, who may need to have a leg amputated

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. Dick also converts a stone barn into a flat for Angel's parents, getting rid of 150 years' worth of rubbish in the process, but tensions rise between the couple when he gets carried away with the trees at the front of the property

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon for an update on an inspirational build. Royal Marine captain Jon White and his wife Becky were living happily in a small cottage when in June 2010, while serving in Afghanistan, Jon stepped on an improvised explosive device, losing three limbs. Every part of their lives had to change - starting with where they could live. Their architect came up with a cutting-edge, semi open-plan building with a dramatic wing-like-roof - and crucially no obvious signs of adaptations for Jon's disabilities. Has the new home proved as enabling for the couple and their expanding family as they had hoped?

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Dubbed 'The Beast' by Winston Churchill, Tirpitz was Nazi Germany's most powerful battleship. Protected by an elite squadron of fighters, it was seen as invincible, but after 34 failed attempts to sink it, the RAF sent in the 617 'Dambusters' Squadron and 9 Squadron. In a raid in November 1944 they managed to slip through enemy defences and destroy Tirpitz. With eyewitness accounts from surviving RAF crew, one of the Luftwaffe fighter pilots and German sailors, this documentary tells this extraordinary story and offers new evidence to explain the mission's improbable success

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The fate of German armed military transport ship MV Wilhelm Gustloff, which was sunk in January 1945 by Soviet submarine S-13 in the Baltic Sea while evacuating German civilian refugees. Plus, the attack on Japan's naval base at Micronesia's Chuuk Lagoon in February 1944

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Phil Wang take on guest captain Alan Carr and Harriet Kemsley. David O'Doherty and Joe Wilkinson join wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Warwick, a 46-year-old man is paralysed from his neck down six weeks after major spinal surgery. Paramedic David Hastie and student paramedic Esme Jenkins need to move him with extreme care: one false move could mean he will never walk again. At the same time, paramedics in Birmingham treat a man who has chest pains after being involved in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Dubbed 'The Beast' by Winston Churchill, Tirpitz was Nazi Germany's most powerful battleship. Protected by an elite squadron of fighters, it was seen as invincible, but after 34 failed attempts to sink it, the RAF sent in the 617 'Dambusters' Squadron and 9 Squadron. In a raid in November 1944 they managed to slip through enemy defences and destroy Tirpitz. With eyewitness accounts from surviving RAF crew, one of the Luftwaffe fighter pilots and German sailors, this documentary tells this extraordinary story and offers new evidence to explain the mission's improbable success

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Scotland to find out about a mysterious condition threatening salmon stocks, Kate Quilton heads Down Under to learn about kangaroo farming and Matt Tebbutt goes behind the scenes of the annual race to get new season asparagus onto the nation's supermarket shelves

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco to uncover the secrets of saffron, revealing what makes it the most expensive spice in the world

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers investigate a huge case that has them searching far and wide for rightful heirs. They also successfully reunite a family separated by wartime evacuation. Plus, details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien converts tank parts to garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard. Later, Simon restores a model Hurricane, and Henry goes potty for a retro petrol pump

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to a private railway, where Simon converts a lantern to a stylish table and Henry turns a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp

programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Cameras return as the season comes to an end to see whether a couple of newlyweds have made a success of their new Spanish bar. Plus, a couple from Surrey consider branching out into the B&B business

programme.desc: The competition kicks off in Worcestershire, where friends Geri Atterbury and Lorraine Rigby invite guests to stay at Greenways of Malvern. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Royal Forester at Bewdley in Worcestershire for their second stay, with proprietors Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker hoping to impress their guests with a trip to a local safari park. Sarah and Luke are impressed with their colourful room, but Sanjeev and Elinor have a 'hairy' experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Ferry Lodge in Holyhead, Anglesey, where mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway hope to impress the other contestants. However, things do not get off to a good start when Geri and Lorraine are overwhelmed by the number of laminated signs, Sean and Maxine begrudge paying 50p for toiletries and Sanjeev and Elinor are left unimpressed by the cleanliness of the establishment

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Partners Sanjeev Bhagotra and Elinor Roberts welcome their guests to Court Colman Manor, Bridgend, Glamorgan. Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are pleased with the size of their room on arrival, but Sarah Allaway discovers a collection of cobwebs in hers. During the afternoon, the groups take a lesson in fire-making, and the next morning a traditional Welsh breakfast is not to everyone's taste - with the hosts wondering whether tactical voting has played a part come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one last time to find out what they have been paid. Geri kicks off proceedings by demanding to know why Sarah and Luke did not want a return visit to Greenways of Malvern, before they in turn confront Sanjeev and Elinor over their low cleanliness score for Ferry Lodge. Then the Court Colman Manor proprietors accuse Sarah and Luke of unfairly judging their hotel, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Auckland couple Bridget and Steve Varney take on the challenge of converting the old Farmers Grand Tearooms built in 1930 into a New York-style loft

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie discover their beloved six-year-old German shepherd has a tumour. In desperation, they turn to Noel Fitzpatrick in the hope he can find a way to prolong Otto's life until their wedding day. The team also sees Jane, Simon and their Rottweiler puppy Elvis, who is having trouble walking, and seven-week-old Pomeranian Gucci, brought in with a broken paw by Amber and her two children after only 48 hours in her new home

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. As if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a project to renovate and extend a classic 1920s cinema in Thorne, South Yorkshire. With little knowledge of restoration, Gwyn and Kate boldly set about turning the dilapidated building into a family home using masses of concrete after taking advice from an architect friend. The couple are hoping to create a hydraulic glass wall that opens up one side of the house to form a UV-filtered canopy, and there are also plans for a white roof extension that resembles a diving board

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Coventry, crewmates Mark Rattray and Orlaigh Barbour attend a 73-year-old woman who is in agony after falling on the ice. Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy are sent to a baby who has been fitting. On the other side of the city, Neil Vann and Laura Perkins have to deal with a well-known drunk nuisance caller

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Victoria Coren Mitchell in the words-and-numbers quiz. Doctor-turned-writer Adam Kay joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty takes to the skies above Israel as he joins the battle to protect the bell pepper crop from the Mediterranean fruit fly and explores whether dishwasher salt is suitable to put on chips. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to find out if coconut oil is any healthier than other oils and heads to the Amazon rainforest to investigate rumours that the Brazil nuts on UK supermarket shelves are all radioactive

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his gastronomic road trip from Venice to Istanbul taking in the countries of the former Byzantine Empire - a renowned melting pot of East and West. This instalment finds the gastronome in the city of Ravenna, where he discovers Piadinas, a thin Italian flatbread street food dating back to Roman times. He then crosses the Adriatic Sea to Croatia - a first for Rick - and a gets a chance to discover new a wealth of dishes such as pasticada, goat stew with peas and lamb peka

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Dave is unhappy about Nessa's plans to let Smithy see the baby, while Stacey ensures Gavin sticks to his promise as she tries to find a new job. Meanwhile, Dawn and Pete join Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night. Comedy, starring Joanna Page and Mathew Horne

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian steps out of the spotlight to learn another trade, trying his hand at the distinctly glamourless profession of farming - and his introduction to the job sees him mucking out the cows

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline
programme.desc: The highs and lows of Prince Harry and Prince William's roles in the forces, featuring an interview with Harry in which he discusses a planned secret deployment in Afghanistan

programme.title: Shipwreck Psycho
programme.desc: Forensic scientists search an island off Western Australia where a Dutch ship was wrecked in 1629 - only for one of the survivors to turn on the others, leading to mass murder

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The Vatican is home to classified files that could reveal top-secret ties to the Nazis, the Mafia, and extraterrestrials

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A floating bridge in Seattle, one of the longest such structures in the world, intended to connect the growing suburb of Bellevue with the high-tech downtown heart of the city. Plus, Albert Zwueste is on the Dutch Caribbean island of Curacao exploring one of the world's oldest surviving floating bridges, which helped inspire and inform the Seattle project

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey from Ayr to Skye. He begins by visiting the home town of poet Robert Burns and finds out how to make haggis. He then discovers the way in which the railway transformed the game of golf in Prestwick, and uncovers the story of the great Victorian tartan hoax in Paisley

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey along the west coast of Scotland from Ayr to Skye, stopping off to explore the Dumbarton shipyards that were responsible for the construction of the famous Cutty Sark. He also goes hunting for gold in the mountains, and visits Loch Lomond, one of Queen Victoria's favourite haunts

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their meteoric rise in the 19th Century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering. They've conquered dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities.

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the startling similarities between humans and great apes, meeting an orang-utan able to row boats and wash clothes and a gorilla that can communicate using sign language. He also traces the history of human settlement and considers the future of population control and food production

programme.title: War Above the Trenches
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about the Royal Flying Corps, recreating the aerial struggle of the First World War based on the personal accounts of pilots

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: The impact of Russia pulling out of the war, only for the US to join soon afterwards, and the events in the aftermath that contributed to the Second World War

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Ayr to Skye and investigates the parallel 'roads' etched in the side of Glen Roy - one of the geological mysteries of the 19th century. He also finds out how the Victorians put a weather observatory on the top of Ben Nevis, and crosses the Glenfinnan viaduct by steam train

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts return to Wightwick Manor near Wolverhampton, where members of the public bring along their treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and find out their worth. Items on show include two drawings by JMW Turner - including one found in the manor itself - objects once owned by the last survivor of the Titanic, and a collection of posters celebrating the Spaghetti Western film genre

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates are shocked to discover there is a thief in their midst, so Fletcher decides to stage a mock trial to bring the culprit to justice with the help of Slade's resident legal consultant Judge Rawley. programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates decide on a wager as the parole board gathers, with Blanco odds-on favourite for release. However, the old lag turns down his freedom and leaves Fletcher in danger of losing a small fortune. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

Plus, Rob Collings flies one of the most successful aircraft of the Second World War, the distinctive P38, former RAF test pilot Andy Edgell tracks down the product of a top secret research project, the X-14 and retired US Air Force pilot Mace Carpenter reconnects with the stealthy F117

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh

Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger

programme.desc: Vern and Marko are at crisis point and Rick takes to the air in a race to peg a new lease. Meanwhile, Alex hunts down poachers before a sunset wedding on his Quartz Hill lease

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard visits a dealer specialising in house clearances, and in Sheffield spots industrial furniture at a 19th-century factory. Later, he finds old punk collectable

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At the Port of Algeciras, a man tries to drive away while the Civil Guard inspect his lorry. Plus, officers question suspected drug mules in Barcelona

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: At Madrid Airport, a husband storms off when his wife can't pay their tax bill. Plus, the Civil Guard uncovers fake branded clothes and jewellery

programme.title: Helicopter ER

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: The manufacture of cable cars, how a brand of vodka is made from grass, and the functionality of social networking sites

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Transforming weeds into walls using a substance made out of hemp and concrete. Plus, how skydiving suits help people fly, and the production of the Swedish fish dish surstromming

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Ant Anstead drag a rare 1972 Datsun 510 2-door into the 21st century. The engine is in good order, but the bad paint job, gearbox and bodywork let it down

programme.desc: Helen brings in a classic '80s BMW E30 and plans one of the garage's most incredible builds yet, augmenting it with a wide-arch body-kit for a menacing post-apocalyptic vibe

programme.desc: The remaining crew members at OCC build a bike for the Saginaw Chippewa tribe after the departure of Paul Jr and Mikey

programme.title: Fantomworks

programme.title: Mega Shippers
programme.desc: Mark has to offload 2,800 tonnes of timber before the weather turns, while Chris transports a 57-metre-long bridge section

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: A look at how stagecoaches, road reflectors and custom motorcycle tanks are made and how manufacturing processes have developed over the years

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: How replica clay pipes, drinking fountains, orange liqueur and compound bows are made

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Perfectionist Michelle and her husband, Bryan Adams tribute singer Russell, host first in Cheltenham, on a night when a budding bromance starts to develop. On night two Lydia and Calum have a rustic theme. While their food doesn't seem to quite hit the mark, Calum's impromptu am dram performances do. Finally, Jane and Paul's menu celebrates Jane's favourite things, but gets mixed reviews

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Colin and Elspeth from Edinburgh search for a holiday home in Menorca, which they can enjoy well into their retirement with their family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Full-time academic Rosie from Ascot in Berkshire is hoping to find the perfect getaway on the Croatian Island of Hvar. Ben Hillman shows her a selection within a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wiltshire beauty salon owners Errol and Theresa Chandiram have plans to retire on the Costa Blanca. Ben Hillman shows them five properties around the town of Ciudad Quesada for their budget of £72,000, but will any of them live up to their dreams?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five amateur cooks from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, take turns trying to throw the perfect dinner party. Retired businesswoman Angie Battye hosts the first night and hopes her Asian roots and love of Barbadian cuisine will impress her guests. She also provides entertainment in the form of limbo dancing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT expert and disco fanatic Joel Rowbottom throws the second of the week's parties in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with an exciting mixture of food and dance. The host faces awkward questions about his disco pants, resulting in some frank revelations, but hopes he can turn the evening's fortunes around with his 'dad dancing' competition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Donna Stephenson hopes to impress with a main course of 'bunny delight' as she hosts the third dinner party from Wakefield, West Yorkshire - but her guests are distracted by the number of animals she has

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shoeless window cleaner Jake Coxon hosts the fourth dinner party from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with beef Wellington, and performing his song God Is a Rocking Chair with his band the Sourheads

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Francesca Saynor hosts the final dinner party in Wakefield, hoping that her simple menu and back-to-school theme will be enough to impress her guests and make her the winner of the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sandra Dee's, a seafood and barbecue restaurant in desperate need of a renovation in Sacramento, California. His team have just 24 hours to refurbish the establishment, and Gordon creates an exciting new menu by incorporating existing dishes and elevating them to a higher standard. Will returning customers be impressed by the changes?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Mariam and Johnno restore their chateau's original bread oven for a sourdough weekend. Angel goes bargain hunting to find a unique radiator cover for the chateau's boot room, while Emma and Paul complete the final room in the west wing and theme it after the renowned artist Alphonse Mucha.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies shows Gary and Linda five properties within their budget of £85,000 to choose between, in the area around Manilva in southern Spain. Buying a home to share with their wider family has been a long-held dream of the Lincolnshire couple

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: A wheelwright and his team restore a Victorian bicycle, and a couple compete in a regatta to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the Fowey River Class sailing dinghy. An artisan brewer and wine-maker searches for ingredients for his next creation on the grounds of Torbay's most historic building, and a stonemason and drystone waller gets to work on one of Cornwall's most unique landmarks

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The patisserie chefs face chocolate week, beginning with a sharing dessert using both chocolate and a favourite ingredient of judge Cherish Finden. For the showpiece, the teams must construct chocolate showpieces on a house-of-horrors theme, including a moving part, along with 48 smaller treats - all in just five hours. Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The South Yorkshire Police deal with complex cases of parents living in fear of their own children. In Sheffield, a mother asks for her 15-year-old to be taken into custody after he starts causing damage at their home, and for the second time in three days, the same officers are despatched to a report of a son attacking his father. Elsewhere, a distraught mother is under siege from her violent and aggressive 23-year-old son

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The realities of policing home drinking, and what happens when parties go very badly wrong, seen through the eyes of South Yorkshire Police's frontline officers. PCs Alice Marshall and Matt Spaven investigate a house party that's a concern for neighbours, while PCs Mohammed Alrobaidi and Bernice Gott find themselves at risk at a rowdy house party in a quiet street. In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford assess the week's week's biggest news stories, hard-hitting documentaries, laughable comedies and gritty dramas

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: The documentary focusing on various aspects of the legal system follows three of Hampshire's prolific repeat offenders, who are all trying to reform their lives after their release from prison. They are supported by probation and police, doing the best they can, but large caseloads and a lack of resources leave officers overwhelmed - and when it's the job of probation to manage and assess the risk posed by offenders in the community, the consequences of mistakes can be disastrous

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

In Worcester, paramedics Ed Hird and Nick Carson have to negotiate a garden fence to reach a 65-year-old biker with a shattered leg

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A doctor shares his story of leaving his family and newborn child during lockdown to keep them safe. An 11-year-old hit by a car shows signs of possible internal bleeding, and a 60-year-old woman is injured after tripping over her cat. A footballer being treated for injuries to his achilles tendon describes how the sport has been an escape for him during his childhood in south London

programme.title: A Very British Sex Shop
programme.desc: Documentary following the Richardsons, who run a chain of sex shops in Brighton and whose day-to-day dealings provide a unique insight into the bedroom habits of Britain. The film charts the family's efforts to keep business booming, from brainstorms on making the most out of the lucrative Valentine's season to a latex fashion show, starring owner Tim's daughter Kizzy

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: As the group enjoy sunny Almancil in the Algarve, Brendan brings them crashing down to earth by announcing there will be a whole week's worth of twists

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, Gillian and husband Steve kick things off with a simple menu and put the emphasis on presentation. Day two sees Holly and Richard lay on a vegetarian meal, which fails to please Gillian. Foodies Jen and Alex host the final night, featuring some rap poetry

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Darrell from south-west London are looking for a holiday home on the Balearic Island of Menorca to enjoy with their two young children and Darrell's grown-up son. They run their own busy human resources company together so cannot wait to find a bolthole to relax in and have some quality time as a family. With a budget of £300,000 Danni Menzies is on hand to show them five properties around the south of the island

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee and Lynn Fox from South Yorkshire have been regular visitors to the Tuscan city of Lucca since Lee's job as a mechanical engineer first took him there over 10 years ago. Lynn's hairdressing business and Lee's job keep the couple very busy, so making the most of their quality time together is key. Laura Hamilton makes it her mission to show them a variety of properties as close to the city centre as possible, on a budget of £170,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Richard Negus hosts the first dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, focusing on a 'day at the races' theme. He serves a course called Under Starter's Orders, and They're Off-al for his main course, and provides a game of snail racing for entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business studies lecturer Amber Elba Porter hosts the second dinner party in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, hoping her spiritual-themed evening, complete with tarot card readings and rune stones, will win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tim Reinman presents the third dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, but is upstaged by domestic goddess Hannah Totman, leaving spiritualist Amber Elba-Porter and hypnotherapist Sue Smith less than happy with their fellow guest. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue Smith hosts the fourth dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, and decides to organise an evening of entertainment revolving around a 'back to school' theme. However, the items on her menu puzzle the guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Surrey couple Debbie and Simon move into their fairy-tale castle, while Belinda and Lee expand their events portfolio by hosting a classic car weekend

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Poppy are hoping to retire to France's Lot-et-Garonne region, so Ben Hillman sets out to find them the perfect place for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Allan have been together for 10 years and with three grown up children between them, are hoping to buy their first home together - somewhere they can go to relax and unwind in Manilva, Spain. programme.title: Big Dog Britain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Gary and Susie Gillhooly, who want to start a new adventure in Florida's beautiful Sarasota Keys. For their budget of £220,000 they're after a place to escape the British winters, as close to the amazing beaches of the Gulf of Mexico as possible. Jonnie shows them how far their budget will stretch in this highly desirable corner of the US

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retirement is fast approaching for Chris Wood and Eleanor Clements, who are looking for a holiday home on the coast of north Tenerife where they can spend the winter months. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Project manager Yemi Christopher-Soares hosts the third dinner party in north Kent, where she hopes the guests will enjoy her Nigerian-inspired menu and appreciate her love of all things spicy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Party-loving Lisa Townend hosts the final dinner party in north Kent, and hopes that a menu full of French fancies and innovative entertainment in the form of human roulette, will be enough to impress her guests and see her crowned winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: The architect meets a father and son building an ambitious clifftop retreat and a young architect embarking on his first commission - a garden pavilion for his grandmother. programme.title: The Super Orgasm
programme.desc: For most women multiple orgasms can seem almost mythical. But some can experience it not once, twice or even 10 times, but 60, 70 or 100 - a super-orgasm. In this documentary, a group of these women volunteer for a series of tests in a scientific investigation to reveal the truth behind the super-orgasm. The aim is to measure and record the phenomenon for the first time, finding out how the brain and the body react before, during and after, and asking if these women are wired differently to other people. And if not, is it something that could be learned by anyone?

programme.desc: Publicly shamed, shunned by her university friends and out of Lady Parts, Amina is at an all-time low, so is grateful when Noor offers to help her get back on track - firstly by finding a respectable man to marry who will overlook her transgressions. But unaware to the couple the host has recently gone vegan, so it turns out to be a meat-free knees-up. Kim and Matt reopen their chip shop after a devastating fire which forced them to shut for a year and Harrison is down on the marina, trying to sell tickets for the Black Pearl pirate ship tour

programme.desc: Well-off Tony and Sarah Page from West Yorkshire leave their eight-bedroom house behind to trade places with the Hollands, who live in a three-bedroom council house in a Hartlepool, and are in the country's poorest 10 per cent. They swap homes, budgets and lives for seven days as they see what it is like at the other end of the economic spectrum

programme.title: Filthy House SOS

An apparently routine stop takes a serious turn as the vehicle pulled over is thought to contain a firearm and the unarmed officers must hold their nerve as they wait for a specialist team to join them. Jamie Theakston narrates

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A high-speed chase in Bradford is ended when CS gas is used to bring a determined speeder to justice, while teams are out on quiet back roads as they track two cars that are thought to be connected with various firearms offences. Plus, burglars find out they cannot outrun the cops when weighed down with a quad bike and trailing a cloud of cannabis behind them

programme.title: Police Interceptors
